workout.description: Full Body workout focusing on strength and endurance training.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift High Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend knees to grip the bar with hands inside your legs, keeping chest up and back flat.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outward at 30-45 degrees, with the barbell over your midfoot.
Hinge at the hips and bend knees to grip the bar with hands inside your legs, keeping chest up and back flat.
Brace your core, drive through your heels, and extend hips and knees simultaneously to lift the bar.
Stand tall at the top with hips fully extended, shoulders back, without hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips and bending the knees, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
exercises.description: A compound lower-body pulling exercise performed from a wide (sumo) stance. The wide foot placement with toes turned out emphasizes the inner thighs and allows for a more upright torso position compared to conventional deadlifts. This variation effectively targets the quads, glutes, and adductors while reducing lower back stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
instructions: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to remove slack before initiating the pull (scapular set).
Exhale and pull your elbows down and back, driving the elbows toward your hips while focusing on pulling with the lats rather than just the arms.
Continue the pull until your chin clears or reaches the level of the handles and your chest approaches the grips; pause and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second.
Inhale and lower yourself slowly and under control back to a full hang with arms fully extended, maintaining shoulder tension and avoiding a sudden drop.
Keep the torso stable and avoid excessive swinging or kipping; if needed, use a controlled bend in the knees or a slight leg cross to prevent momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on smooth tempo, full range of motion, and consistent breathing.
exercises.description: A narrow, neutral-grip chin-up performed from a vertical hang that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the arms and upper back. Pull the body up until the chin clears the handles, then lower under control to a full hang.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width), fingers splayed, and body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Brace your core and glutes to maintain a rigid body position with neutral spine and neck.
Lower your body by bending the elbows outward until your chest is close to the floor or elbows reach approximately 90 degrees.
Hold this lowered position isometrically, maintaining full-body tension and keeping hips level without sagging or piking.
Breathe steadily while holding for the desired duration, then press back up to complete the set.
exercises.description: An isometric hold variation of the wide hand push-up where you maintain a static lowered position with hands placed wider than shoulder width. This hold maximizes time under tension for the pectorals and core stabilizers while maintaining a rigid body line.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.notes: Extend your body into a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core and glutes to maintain a rigid plank position throughout the movement.
instructions: Place your feet on a bench or elevated surface and position your hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Extend your body into a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core and glutes to maintain a rigid plank position throughout the movement.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows at a 30-45 degree angle from your torso, keeping your head neutral and spine aligned.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms without locking the elbows.
Maintain core tension throughout and avoid letting your hips sag or pike up during the movement.
exercises.description: A decline push-up performed with feet elevated on a bench, placing greater emphasis on the upper chest and anterior deltoids. The elevated foot position increases the difficulty compared to standard push-ups while targeting the pectorals, with secondary engagement from the triceps, anterior delts, serratus anterior, and core for stabilization.

exercises.name: Landmine Shoulders Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neutral spine, and position the bar angled away from you so it can travel upward without hitting your head.
Inhale, then press the handles upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your arms are fully extended overhead or slightly behind the head, avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and elbows unlocked but not hyperextended.
Exhale as you slowly lower the handles back down to the starting shoulder-height position under control, maintaining tension in the delts and core.
Repeat for the desired reps; keep movement smooth and controlled, avoid leaning the torso excessively back or using lower-body drive to move the load.
Adjust stance or hand position to shift emphasis (wider grip = more lateral delt emphasis; closer grip = more anterior delt/pectorals) and lower the weight if you cannot maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A standing landmine press using a Viking press attachment that emphasizes overhead pressing from the shoulders; the movement drives the handles upward from shoulder height to full arm extension, targeting the delts while the triceps and upper chest assist.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width), fingers splayed, and body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Brace your core and glutes to maintain a rigid body position with neutral spine and neck.
Lower your body by bending the elbows outward until your chest is close to the floor or elbows reach approximately 90 degrees.
Hold this lowered position isometrically, maintaining full-body tension and keeping hips level without sagging or piking.
Breathe steadily while holding for the desired duration, then press back up to complete the set.
exercises.description: An isometric hold variation of the wide hand push-up where you maintain a static lowered position with hands placed wider than shoulder width. This hold maximizes time under tension for the pectorals and core stabilizers while maintaining a rigid body line.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
With arms relatively straight, lift the barbell backward and upward away from your body by squeezing the rear delts.
Raise the bar as high as comfortable while maintaining control, then lower slowly back to the starting position.
Avoid leaning forward or using momentum; focus on controlled movement through the rear deltoids.
exercises.description: A standing barbell exercise performed with the bar held behind the body using an overhand grip. The movement involves lifting the barbell backward and upward away from the glutes by extending the shoulders, keeping arms relatively straight to target the posterior deltoids and traps.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Lever Alternating Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
Load an appropriate plate weight and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp the right and left lever handles (one in each hand) with a neutral or overhand grip. Begin with both arms extended overhead with a slight bend in the elbows.

For each rep, exhale and pull one lever down and in a smooth arc toward your chest/upper stomach using the shoulder joint — lead with the upper arm, not the wrist, and keep the elbow slightly bent and fixed.
At the bottom of the movement, pause briefly and focus on squeezing the pectorals and lats on the working side while keeping your torso stationary.
Inhale and slowly return the lever under control back to the start (overhead) to complete the eccentric phase; avoid letting the shoulder snap back.
Alternate sides each repetition (right arm then left arm) while maintaining the same tempo, core bracing, and no excessive torso rotation or lumbar arch.
Keep movements controlled, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain a fixed elbow angle and stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover is a single-arm/alternating machine pullover that moves the upper arm in an arc from overhead down toward the chest, emphasizing the pectorals while also recruiting the lats and serratus anterior. The movement is performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away, unrack the bar and position it directly above your upper chest with arms extended.
Inhale and lower the bar with control to your upper chest near the collarbone, keeping elbows at a 45-60 degree angle from your torso.
Press the bar back up explosively while exhaling, extending your arms fully without locking the elbows aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the barbell with control.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
instructions: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to remove slack before initiating the pull (scapular set).
Exhale and pull your elbows down and back, driving the elbows toward your hips while focusing on pulling with the lats rather than just the arms.
Continue the pull until your chin clears or reaches the level of the handles and your chest approaches the grips; pause and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second.
Inhale and lower yourself slowly and under control back to a full hang with arms fully extended, maintaining shoulder tension and avoiding a sudden drop.
Keep the torso stable and avoid excessive swinging or kipping; if needed, use a controlled bend in the knees or a slight leg cross to prevent momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on smooth tempo, full range of motion, and consistent breathing.
exercises.description: A narrow, neutral-grip chin-up performed from a vertical hang that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the arms and upper back. Pull the body up until the chin clears the handles, then lower under control to a full hang.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.notes: Place a bench or sturdy platform behind you and kneel so your knees and lower legs rest on the bench (body facing the floor) — this creates the decline angle.
instructions: Place a bench or sturdy platform behind you and kneel so your knees and lower legs rest on the bench (body facing the floor) — this creates the decline angle.
Take a shoulder-width hand placement on the floor in front of you, fingers spread for stability; extend your arms so your torso forms a straight line from head to knees.
Brace your core and glutes to keep the spine neutral (avoid sagging hips or a piked butt); retract your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the upper back.
Inhale and lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows, keeping them at about a 30–45° angle from the torso, until elbows are roughly parallel to the floor or reach a comfortable depth.
Exhale and press through your palms to straighten the arms and return to the start position, maintaining a rigid body line throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, controlling both the descent and ascent; if form breaks (hips sag or shoulders rise), stop and reset.
exercises.description: A kneeling decline push-up places the feet/knees on an elevated surface so the upper body moves at a steeper angle, increasing load on the upper chest. It is a bodyweight pressing exercise that also challenges the shoulders and core for stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and place a loaded barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip (wider than shoulder-width) and let your arms hang fully extended.
Brace your core, inhale, then pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows out and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders retracted, then exhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner until arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or spinal rounding; keep knees slightly bent and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed with a wide overhand grip that emphasizes the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. The torso stays hinged forward while the hips and spine stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
instructions: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to remove slack before initiating the pull (scapular set).
Exhale and pull your elbows down and back, driving the elbows toward your hips while focusing on pulling with the lats rather than just the arms.
Continue the pull until your chin clears or reaches the level of the handles and your chest approaches the grips; pause and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second.
Inhale and lower yourself slowly and under control back to a full hang with arms fully extended, maintaining shoulder tension and avoiding a sudden drop.
Keep the torso stable and avoid excessive swinging or kipping; if needed, use a controlled bend in the knees or a slight leg cross to prevent momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on smooth tempo, full range of motion, and consistent breathing.
exercises.description: A narrow, neutral-grip chin-up performed from a vertical hang that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the arms and upper back. Pull the body up until the chin clears the handles, then lower under control to a full hang.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.notes: Place a bench or sturdy platform behind you and kneel so your knees and lower legs rest on the bench (body facing the floor) — this creates the decline angle.
instructions: Place a bench or sturdy platform behind you and kneel so your knees and lower legs rest on the bench (body facing the floor) — this creates the decline angle.
Take a shoulder-width hand placement on the floor in front of you, fingers spread for stability; extend your arms so your torso forms a straight line from head to knees.
Brace your core and glutes to keep the spine neutral (avoid sagging hips or a piked butt); retract your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the upper back.
Inhale and lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows, keeping them at about a 30–45° angle from the torso, until elbows are roughly parallel to the floor or reach a comfortable depth.
Exhale and press through your palms to straighten the arms and return to the start position, maintaining a rigid body line throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, controlling both the descent and ascent; if form breaks (hips sag or shoulders rise), stop and reset.
exercises.description: A kneeling decline push-up places the feet/knees on an elevated surface so the upper body moves at a steeper angle, increasing load on the upper chest. It is a bodyweight pressing exercise that also challenges the shoulders and core for stability.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.notes: Extend your body into a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core and glutes to maintain a rigid plank position throughout the movement.
instructions: Place your feet on a bench or elevated surface and position your hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Extend your body into a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core and glutes to maintain a rigid plank position throughout the movement.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows at a 30-45 degree angle from your torso, keeping your head neutral and spine aligned.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms without locking the elbows.
Maintain core tension throughout and avoid letting your hips sag or pike up during the movement.
exercises.description: A decline push-up performed with feet elevated on a bench, placing greater emphasis on the upper chest and anterior deltoids. The elevated foot position increases the difficulty compared to standard push-ups while targeting the pectorals, with secondary engagement from the triceps, anterior delts, serratus anterior, and core for stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever Alternating Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
Load an appropriate plate weight and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp the right and left lever handles (one in each hand) with a neutral or overhand grip. Begin with both arms extended overhead with a slight bend in the elbows.

For each rep, exhale and pull one lever down and in a smooth arc toward your chest/upper stomach using the shoulder joint — lead with the upper arm, not the wrist, and keep the elbow slightly bent and fixed.
At the bottom of the movement, pause briefly and focus on squeezing the pectorals and lats on the working side while keeping your torso stationary.
Inhale and slowly return the lever under control back to the start (overhead) to complete the eccentric phase; avoid letting the shoulder snap back.
Alternate sides each repetition (right arm then left arm) while maintaining the same tempo, core bracing, and no excessive torso rotation or lumbar arch.
Keep movements controlled, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain a fixed elbow angle and stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover is a single-arm/alternating machine pullover that moves the upper arm in an arc from overhead down toward the chest, emphasizing the pectorals while also recruiting the lats and serratus anterior. The movement is performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row

instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight up over your chest with elbows bent roughly 90 degrees; keep the elbows fixed throughout the movement.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the shoulders before beginning the descent.

Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing shoulder extension (keeping the elbow angle constant) until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest area or until the bar reaches a comfortable range of motion.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and serratus anterior, then pull the bar back over the chest by driving the shoulders into flexion using your lats and chest (exhale on the concentric).
Keep the movement smooth and controlled, avoiding excessive lower back arch; maintain the bent-elbow position and avoid turning the elbows outward or using momentum.
Perform the desired number of reps and then safely rack the barbell, or have a spotter assist when lowering the weight off your chest.
exercises.description: A supine barbell pullover performed with the elbows bent that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, stretching and loading the latissimus region while also recruiting the chest and triceps. Performed with a fixed elbow angle to emphasize shoulder extension and thoracic mobility.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Landmine Neutral Grip Chest Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
instructions: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
Position: Lie supine on the floor (or on a flat bench) with knees bent and feet flat; align your chest under the handles so the bar starts near your sternum.
Grip: Reach up and grasp the neutral handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands roughly shoulder-width apart.
Brace: Take a breath, brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Press: Drive the handles upward and slightly forward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (do not force a hard lockout).
Pause & control: Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position under control until they touch or come close to your chest.
Reps & safety notes: Repeat for the desired reps. Keep feet planted, avoid excessive low-back arch, and maintain scapular control throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A landmine chest press performed from a supine position using a Viking press attachment and a neutral (palms-facing) grip. It emphasizes horizontal pressing mechanics to load the pectorals while the neutral grip reduces shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a loaded barbell within reach on the floor or on low supports.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a loaded barbell within reach on the floor or on low supports.
Lie face down (prone) on the bench so your chest is supported, feet braced on the floor or bench for stability, and your torso is fixed against the pad.
Reach down and grasp the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width apart. Allow your arms to hang fully extended.
Before pulling, draw your shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to start the movement from the back rather than the arms.
Pull the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows back and slightly out.
At the top, squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold briefly (about 1 second) while keeping the torso pressed to the bench and neck neutral.
Lower the barbell slowly back to full arm extension under control, maintaining scapular tension and avoiding any bouncing or trunk rotation.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale while lowering. Keep movement strict—do not jerk the weight or lift your chest off the bench.
exercises.description: Prone row performed on an incline bench where the torso is supported and a barbell is pulled toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. It emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the upper back, rear delts and arm muscles.

exercises.name: Lever Alternating Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
Load an appropriate plate weight and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp the right and left lever handles (one in each hand) with a neutral or overhand grip. Begin with both arms extended overhead with a slight bend in the elbows.

For each rep, exhale and pull one lever down and in a smooth arc toward your chest/upper stomach using the shoulder joint — lead with the upper arm, not the wrist, and keep the elbow slightly bent and fixed.
At the bottom of the movement, pause briefly and focus on squeezing the pectorals and lats on the working side while keeping your torso stationary.
Inhale and slowly return the lever under control back to the start (overhead) to complete the eccentric phase; avoid letting the shoulder snap back.
Alternate sides each repetition (right arm then left arm) while maintaining the same tempo, core bracing, and no excessive torso rotation or lumbar arch.
Keep movements controlled, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain a fixed elbow angle and stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover is a single-arm/alternating machine pullover that moves the upper arm in an arc from overhead down toward the chest, emphasizing the pectorals while also recruiting the lats and serratus anterior. The movement is performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable Upper Chest Crossovers
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (above shoulder height) and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (above shoulder height) and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the stations with a staggered or shoulder-width stance for stability and grab a handle in each hand (palms facing down or slightly rotated), arms out to the sides.
Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso is upright but leaning a bit toward the floor; keep chest up, shoulders down and back, and a slight bend in the elbows throughout.

Exhale and pull both handles in a controlled downward and inward arc, bringing your hands together in front of the upper chest/clavicle area (hands may cross slightly) while actively squeezing the pectorals.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding shoulder rotation or shrugging.
Inhale and slowly return the handles along the same arc back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it pull you back.
Keep core engaged and avoid using momentum; choose a weight that allows full range of motion with good form.
exercises.description: Standing cable crossovers performed with the pulleys set high to emphasize the upper chest; the movement brings the handles down and together in a downward arc to target the clavicular portion of the pectorals. Maintain a slight forward lean and a small bend in the elbows while squeezing the chest at the finish.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Pec Fly
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine handles so they are roughly at mid-chest/shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the lever machine handles so they are roughly at mid-chest/shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand centered between the lever arms with feet about shoulder-width apart and a soft bend in the knees; maintain a neutral spine and a slight forward lean from the hips.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or palms-in grip and raise your arms out to the sides so they are roughly parallel to the floor; maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout (start position).
Exhale and bring the handles together in front of your chest by squeezing the pectorals, keeping the elbow angle constant and avoiding shoulder shrugging or excessive forward elbow travel.
Pause briefly at peak contraction with hands meeting or close in front of the chest, focusing on contracting the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly control the levers back to the start position until you feel a stretch across the chest; do not let the weights slam back.
Repeat for the prescribed reps. Keep shoulders down and back, core braced, and avoid rotating the torso or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing machine lever pec fly that isolates the chest by bringing two arms together in front of the body from a wide, horizontal start. Performed standing with a slight forward lean to maximize pectoral contraction while the delts and serratus anterior assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Neutral grip Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
Stand between the stacks in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) and take one handle in each hand with palms facing each other.
Step forward slightly until you feel tension in the cables, lean your torso forward a few degrees and keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to prevent trunk rotation or excessive arching.
Exhale and bring your hands together in a controlled horizontal arc in front of your chest, squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Hold the contracted position for 1–2 seconds, then inhale and slowly reverse the motion, letting the cables return your arms to the starting wide position while maintaining the elbow bend.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding locking the elbows or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly using a neutral (palms-facing) grip that isolates the chest by bringing the hands together in a horizontal arc. Performed from a staggered stance with a slight elbow bend to emphasize the pectoral squeeze while the cables provide continuous tension.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Single Arm Fly
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to mid-chest height and select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to mid-chest height and select a moderate weight.
Stand side-on to the machine with feet staggered (one foot slightly forward) for a stable base and brace your core (standing position).
Reach across and grab the handle with the working hand, palm facing in or slightly up; allow the arm to start out to the side with a small, natural bend in the elbow (not locked).
Retract the shoulder blade slightly and maintain a tall posture with shoulders down; keep the non-working arm relaxed or placed on your hip for balance.
Exhale and bring the hand across your body in a wide arc, driving horizontal adduction from the shoulder so the hand finishes in front of the chest or slightly across midline.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while keeping the elbow slightly bent and the torso stable (avoid rotating or leaning excessively).
Inhale and slowly resist the cable as you return the arm back to the starting position with control, feeling the stretch in the pectoral at the end range.
Repeat for desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain core bracing and a fixed slight elbow bend throughout to protect the joint and target the pectorals.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable fly performs a horizontal adduction movement of the shoulder to isolate the chest (pectorals) unilaterally while the core stabilizes the torso. The exercise emphasizes controlled inward movement of the arm across the body with a slight elbow bend.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Fly
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
Select an appropriate weight and sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest up and shoulders down and back.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and position your upper arms roughly parallel to the floor with a small bend in the elbows.
Start with the handles out to the sides so your chest is stretched but shoulders are not overstretched; maintain a stable torso against the back pad.
Exhale and drive the handles together in a smooth horizontal arc by contracting the pectorals, bringing your hands close together or slightly in front of the chest.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, squeezing the chest while keeping elbows slightly bent and avoiding shoulder shrugging.
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to return to the start position until you feel a controlled stretch in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding leaning forward or using momentum to move the weight.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly that brings the arms together in a horizontal arc to isolate and contract the pectoral muscles. Performed on a lever/pec-deck machine with the chest supported and elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Sit on the pulldown bench with knees secured under the thigh pads and feet flat on the floor (seated, knees braced).
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin tall with a neutral spine and a slight lean back from the hips (not from the lower back), chest lifted and eyes forward.
Inhale, then exhale and initiate the movement by pulling the shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to engage the lats.
Pull the bar down in a controlled arc toward the upper chest (sternal area), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with straight arms, fully extending the shoulders while maintaining a stable torso.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum; keep movement controlled and driven by the lats.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar performed from a tall seated position, pulling the bar down to the upper chest to target the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Focus is on scapular retraction and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand facing a low cable pulley with feet about hip-width apart and attach a straight bar or handle to the low setting.
instructions: Stand facing a low cable pulley with feet about hip-width apart and attach a straight bar or handle to the low setting.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angle), keeping a slight bend in the knees; this is a bent-over position.
Maintain a neutral spine and head in line with the torso, bracing your core and keeping the chest lifted throughout the set.
Reach forward and grab the bar with a comfortable grip (overhand or neutral), allowing the cable to pull your arms fully extended.
Initiate the row by driving your elbows straight back toward your hips, squeezing the shoulder blades together and leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at full contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding any excessive torso rotation or momentum.
Slowly extend your arms under control back to the start position until the lats are stretched and the shoulder blades open, inhale on the return.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements controlled and spine neutral; reduce weight if you feel lower back rounding or excessive swinging.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with a low cable pulley that targets the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades while the torso is hinged forward. The movement also engages the lats, arms and posterior shoulder while the posterior chain and core stabilize the body.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
instructions: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; maintain a neutral spine and soft knees.
Keep your torso upright and immobile; do not lean or use leg drive throughout the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears by driving the scapulae upward—think of trying to touch your shoulders to your ears.
Hold the top position and squeeze the traps and levator scapulae for 1–2 seconds while keeping the arms straight and grip firm (forearms engaged).
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders and avoid excessive neck extension—keep your head neutral and eyes forward.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs target the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles; the lift is a vertical scapular elevation with a controlled lowering phase.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
Brace your core, keep the elbow straight (but not locked) and shoulders relaxed before initiating the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) the shoulder of the working arm straight up toward the ear, using only the shoulder girdle—do not roll or rotate the shoulder.
Pause and squeeze the traps at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on the contraction near the neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulder back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended and the trap is relaxed.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch arms; maintain neutral head position and avoid using momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm dumbbell shrug that elevates the shoulder to target the upper trapezius and surrounding neck muscles. Performed with one arm at a time to concentrate on trap contraction and improve unilateral strength and symmetry.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
