exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench with knees secured under the pads and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a neutral spine and chest slightly lifted; lean back only a few degrees (small recline) — do not arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/clavicle while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Hold and squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement without leaning excessively backward.
Slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the start position until the arms are nearly fully extended and the shoulder blades re-expand.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled tempo and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar targets the latissimus dorsi with a wide overhand grip, pulling the bar down toward the upper chest. The movement also engages the biceps, rear delts and upper back muscles to stabilize and complete the pull.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arms Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your head, shoulders and upper back supported, feet flat on the floor and hips pressed down (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your head, shoulders and upper back supported, feet flat on the floor and hips pressed down (supine position).
Grasp a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral or palms-facing grip and extend the arm straight above your chest with a slight bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs and keep your ribs down to prevent excessive lower-back arching throughout the movement.

Slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing the shoulder to extend, keeping the elbow slightly bent and fixed in angle; stop when you feel a comfortable stretch in the lats/upper chest or when the upper arm is roughly parallel with the bench.
Pause briefly at the stretch, then contract the lats and chest to pull the dumbbell back in the same arc to the start position above the chest while exhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch arms. Maintain slow, controlled tempo and avoid shrugging the shoulder or using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm dumbbell pullover performed lying on a bench where the weight is moved in an arc from above the chest to behind the head, emphasizing shoulder extension to load the lats and upper chest while the core stabilizes the body.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise Wrist Straps with Back Support
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.

The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent (or seated on a bench); keep a tall spine and neutral head position.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing toward you) and extend your arms so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by pulling the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and keeping them at about shoulder height.
As you pull, externally rotate your shoulders so the ends of the rope separate and your hands move either side of your face; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end range.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive backward lean or shrugging.
Slowly return the rope to the start position with control, maintaining tension in the cable and scapular control throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and maintain controlled tempo to protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: A seated cable face pull using a rope attachment that emphasizes the rear delts and upper posterior shoulder musculature by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the hands and squeezing the shoulder blades.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Zottman Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart, knees soft, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-forward/hammer) grip at your sides.

exercises.name: Cable Jack Hammer Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a short straight bar, rope, or hammer-style handle and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Curl
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Zottman Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart, knees soft, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-forward/hammer) grip at your sides.
instructions: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart, knees soft, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-forward/hammer) grip at your sides.
Keep your elbows tucked to your sides and your torso stable; brace your core to prevent swinging.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up by flexing the elbows while simultaneously supinating the forearms so your palms face up at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top with full elbow flexion and palms facing upward.
Rotate your wrists so your palms face down (pronated) and inhale as you slowly lower the weights under control back to the starting position.
Switch the grip back to neutral at the bottom and repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell curl variation that works the biceps during the concentric (curl) while rotating the wrists so the eccentric (lowering) emphasizes the forearms. It combines supination on the way up and pronation on the way down for balanced arm development.

exercises.name: Cable Jack Hammer Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a short straight bar, rope, or hammer-style handle and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a short straight bar, rope, or hammer-style handle and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about hip-width apart and take a neutral (palms facing each other) hammer grip on the handle; lean slightly forward at the hips for balance.
Tuck your elbows to your sides and keep them fixed there for the entire set; your upper arms should remain stationary and perpendicular to the floor.
Exhale and press the handle down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; avoid flaring the elbows forward.
Pause and squeeze the triceps at full extension (1 second), then inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position with control until the forearms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Maintain a controlled tempo (e.g., 1–2 seconds down, 1–2 seconds up), keep the torso stable and abs braced, and repeat for desired reps.
Avoid using body momentum or excessive shoulder movement; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stationary or if you feel strain in the wrists.
exercises.description: A standing cable pushdown performed with a neutral (hammer) grip that emphasizes elbow extension to load the triceps while minimizing shoulder movement. Use a high pulley with a short straight bar or rope/hammer attachment and keep the elbows locked at the sides throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Curl
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and shoulders back; hold the handle in one hand with a supinated (palm-up) grip.
Keep the working elbow tucked against your side throughout the movement and maintain a neutral wrist.
Exhale and curl the handle upward by flexing the elbow until your biceps are fully contracted and the hand is near shoulder level; avoid swinging the torso or using shoulder elevation.
Pause briefly and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the handle back to full elbow extension under control, maintaining tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch arms. Maintain a steady tempo and avoid using body momentum.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm curl performed on a low cable pulley to isolate the biceps with constant tension. The exercise uses a supinated grip to curl the handle from full elbow extension to shoulder height.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
Lean forward slightly at the hips and place the back of your upper arm (triceps area) or elbow against the inside of the same-side thigh so the elbow is fixed.
Brace your core and keep your upper arm stationary; this is a seated, supported position to isolate the biceps.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the dumbbell approaches shoulder level, focusing on contracting the biceps and keeping the wrist neutral.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top for 1 second without shrugging the shoulder or rocking the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the fully extended starting position with control (eccentric phase).
Avoid using body momentum or moving the upper arm; switch sides after completing the set for the opposite arm.
exercises.description: Seated, single-arm dumbbell curl performed with the working elbow braced against the inside of the same-side thigh to isolate the biceps. The exercise emphasizes a strict concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering to maximize biceps contraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar on the angled (inner) hand positions so your palms are facing upward/neutral; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and core braced to stabilize the spine; elbows should sit close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, driving the movement with your biceps; keep your upper arms stationary and forearms moving.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and biceps are fully contracted; avoid shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full elbow extension without locking the joints.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and strict form throughout.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ bar with a semi-supinated grip to isolate elbow flexion and develop the biceps. Perform controlled concentric and eccentric reps while keeping the torso still to minimize cheating.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (upright torso).
Grab the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing down or slightly toward you), hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the bar at chest/upper-ab level, elbows tucked close to your sides and upper arms stationary (forearms up).
Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging or leaning forward.
Pause briefly at full extension, then inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows while maintaining upper-arm position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping movement controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar that emphasizes elbow extension to work the triceps; performed facing the cable machine with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Keep a neutral spine and controlled tempo, extending the arms fully and returning under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

exercises.name: Cable Rope High Pulley Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set a rope attachment on a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a rope attachment on a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand with your back toward the machine, take a staggered (split) stance for stability, and lean slightly forward at the hips so your torso is angled down but spine remains neutral.
Reach overhead and grasp the rope with both hands in a neutral (thumbs-up) grip, bringing the rope ends beside your head so your upper arms are vertical or slightly forward and elbows pointing up.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms stationary (elbows fixed close to head) throughout the movement.
Exhale and extend your forearms by straightening the elbows, pulling the rope forward/overhead until your arms are fully extended but not aggressively locked out.
Pause briefly at full extension to contract the triceps.
Inhale and slowly allow the rope to return to the start position by bending the elbows, controlling the descent until your forearms are back beside or slightly behind your head.
Repeat for the desired reps while maintaining a neutral wrist position, minimal trunk movement, and steady controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Standing cable rope overhead triceps extension performed from a high pulley; you extend the elbows to work the triceps while the torso is braced in a slight forward lean. The rope allows a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and greater range of motion behind the head.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cross Body Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip, arm fully extended at your side and wrist neutral; the other hand can rest lightly on your hip.
Keep your upper arm and elbow close to your torso and stationary; do not let the elbow travel forward excessively.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell up across your body toward the opposite shoulder in a diagonal path, leading with the thumb while maintaining the neutral grip.
Pause and squeeze at the top for a moment, keeping the wrist straight and the torso stable (do not lean or swing the body).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control, fully extending the elbow but avoiding locking it harshly.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch sides and perform the same steps with the opposite arm.
Maintain steady breathing and a controlled tempo throughout; reduce weight if you must swing or use momentum.
exercises.description: A standing hammer-grip curl where the dumbbell is lifted diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder, emphasizing the biceps with strong forearm involvement. Performed with a neutral (hammer) grip to target the brachialis/brachioradialis region while minimizing wrist rotation.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.

workout.description: Leg Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight. Step under the bar and rest it across your upper back/traps with a secure, pronated grip; unrack the bar and take a small step back.

Position your feet in a split stance: one foot forward (working leg) and the other foot back with toes on the floor and heel elevated slightly; keep a distance so the front knee can bend to about 90 degrees at the bottom.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright and chest lifted throughout the movement; gaze forward to help maintain posture.

Slowly lower your body by bending the front knee and pushing the hips back slightly until the front thigh is around parallel to the floor or you reach a comfortable depth; the rear knee should lower toward the floor but not necessarily touch.
Ensure the front knee tracks in line with your toes (avoid collapsing inward) and keep weight primarily through the front heel and midfoot.
Drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip, returning to the starting split stance. Exhale on the drive.
Complete the desired number of reps on the working leg, then carefully step the back foot forward, re‑rack the bar, reset, and repeat with the opposite leg.
exercises.description: A unilateral, loaded lower‑body exercise performed in a staggered (split) stance with a barbell on the upper back that emphasizes the front leg's quadriceps while also working the glutes and hamstrings for hip control and the core for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward or slightly out, barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
Set a neutral spine (no rounding), pull shoulder blades down and back, lift the chest and brace the abs tightly.
Take a deep breath, drive through the heels and mid-foot, extend the hips and knees together to lift the bar, keeping the bar close to the shins and thighs.
Continue driving the hips forward until standing tall with hips fully extended and shoulders stacked over hips (lockout); do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with glutes engaged and core braced.
To lower, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back while maintaining a neutral spine, let the bar pass the knees, then bend the knees and place the bar back on the floor under control.
Reset breathing and core tension before each repetition and keep consistent form; avoid rounding the spine and jerking the bar.
exercises.description: A heavy compound hip-hinge lift that develops the glutes and the posterior chain by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing position. The movement also requires strong spinal bracing and upper-back stability while challenging grip strength.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Stand with feet hip-width, unrack the bar and take a small step forward so the bar is balanced; shift your weight onto one foot and lift the other foot slightly off the floor or cross it behind the working leg.
Keep the working knee slightly bent (not fully locked) and maintain an upright torso with core engaged.
Exhale and press through the ball of the working foot to rise up as high as possible onto your toes (heel raise), focusing on contracting the calf.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower slowly back down with control until your heel returns to the floor (or to neutral dorsiflexion), keeping balance throughout.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then carefully re-rack the bar if switching legs, or step back under the bar and repeat for the opposite leg.
Maintain steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep shoulders and hips squared forward to prevent compensations.
exercises.description: A unilateral standing calf raise performed inside a Smith machine with the bar resting on the upper back; raises the body onto the ball of one foot to load the calf muscles. Performed on the floor (not a raised step) for balance and single-leg strength and stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
Grasp the handles at chest level (or hold the pad if no handles) and set a moderate weight; maintain a neutral head position and brace your core.

Exhale and contract your abs to curl your torso forward and down, flexing the spine while keeping the hips stationary and using controlled motion — continue until you feel a full contraction of the abs.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement while keeping tension in the abs; do not pull with your arms or yank with momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting upright position with a controlled motion, maintaining core engagement and avoiding hyperextension of the spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Adjust weight to maintain strict form and avoid using neck or hip drive to complete the movement.
exercises.description: A seated, machine-based crunch performed with a chest pad and lever; the exercise flexes the torso forward against resistance to target the rectus abdominis. The knees and feet are stabilized while the upper torso curls forward, emphasizing spinal flexion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.

workout.description: Leg Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
instructions: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
Plant your feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart so that at the top of the movement your knees are roughly bent to 90 degrees; keep chin tucked and gaze forward or slightly up.
Brace your abs and grip the barbell on each side to steady it; maintain a neutral spine (avoid overarching the lower back).
Drive through your heels and contract the glutes to lift your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line (hips fully extended); squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Lower the hips slowly and under control until they are just above the floor or until you feel a stretch in the glutes/hamstrings, maintaining core tension throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement smooth and controlled and avoiding thrusting with the lower back.
If needed, adjust foot distance to emphasize glutes (feet slightly forward) or hamstrings (feet further from bench) while preserving a stable foot contact and neutral spine.
exercises.description: The barbell hip thrust is a supine, bench-supported hip extension exercise that primarily develops the glutes by driving the hips upward against resistance. It also recruits the hamstrings and quads while the core stabilizes the trunk.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
Position your shoulders under the padded lever, stand tall and grip the machine handles for stability; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward (or slightly turned in/out to vary emphasis).
Begin with a full stretch: lower your heels down until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves (keep torso upright and spine neutral).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, exhaling and keeping knees straight (a slight knee bend shifts emphasis to the soleus if desired).
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched starting position with control—avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and proper alignment throughout.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing calf raise where the shoulders are braced under a padded lever and the heels drop and rise from a platform to load the calf muscles through a full range of motion. Emphasizes plantarflexion to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs.

Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back. Retract your shoulder blades and keep them contacting the bench.
Start with the dumbbells at mid-chest, elbows bent about 90 degrees and wrists neutral (dumbbell handles aligned with forearms).
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended; keep wrists stacked over elbows.

Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position, maintaining control and stopping when the dumbbells reach chest level or your elbows form ~90 degrees. Keep elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from your torso to protect the shoulders.
Keep your core braced and feet firmly planted for stability throughout each rep; avoid excessive flaring of the ribs or letting the shoulders lift off the bench.
Use a spotter for heavy loads and choose a weight that allows controlled full-range reps with good form.
exercises.description: Flat dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a bench, pressing two dumbbells upward from chest level to develop the pectorals; the movement also recruits the anterior delts and triceps for stability and lockout.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
