exercises.name: Barbell Bent Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight up over your chest with elbows bent roughly 90 degrees; keep the elbows fixed throughout the movement.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the shoulders before beginning the descent.

Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing shoulder extension (keeping the elbow angle constant) until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest area or until the bar reaches a comfortable range of motion.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and serratus anterior, then pull the bar back over the chest by driving the shoulders into flexion using your lats and chest (exhale on the concentric).
Keep the movement smooth and controlled, avoiding excessive lower back arch; maintain the bent-elbow position and avoid turning the elbows outward or using momentum.
Perform the desired number of reps and then safely rack the barbell, or have a spotter assist when lowering the weight off your chest.
exercises.description: A supine barbell pullover performed with the elbows bent that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, stretching and loading the latissimus region while also recruiting the chest and triceps. Performed with a fixed elbow angle to emphasize shoulder extension and thoracic mobility.

exercises.name: Cable Seated High Row (V-bar)
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar. Sit on the bench facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip and sit upright with a tall chest, slight natural lumbar curve, and shoulders relaxed downward.
Start with your arms extended overhead (or up in front) so there is tension on the cable and your shoulder blades are not fully protracted.
Exhale and pull the V-bar down and back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage and slightly behind you, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction).
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) with your torso stable and not leaning back excessively.
Inhale and slowly control the V-bar back to the start position, allowing the arms to extend while maintaining core tension and posture.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep movements smooth, avoid jerking or using momentum, and do not let the lower back round or overextend.
exercises.description: Seated cable high row using a V-bar is a horizontal/upper-angle pulling movement that targets the muscles of the upper back by driving the elbows down and back while squeezing the shoulder blades together. It uses a neutral grip to emphasize middle traps, rhomboids and lats with assistance from the arms.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shrug
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
instructions: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.

Pick up a barbell with a double overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width to slightly wider, arms fully extended so the bar hangs in front of your thighs; keep your torso neutral and head in a natural position.
Brace your core and maintain a tall, upright posture (no leaning forward or backward) throughout the movement.
Without bending the elbows, lift (shrug) the shoulders straight up toward the ears by elevating the scapulae; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds to maximize contraction of the traps.
Lower the shoulders slowly and under control to the starting position, allowing a full stretch of the traps between reps.
Breathe out as you shrug up and inhale as you lower; perform the desired number of reps while keeping movement strict and controlled.
exercises.description: A seated barbell shrug isolates the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a barbell; performing it seated reduces leg drive so the traps take most of the load. It emphasizes strong, vertical shoulder elevation (scapular elevation) with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley. Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back and grasp the handle with one hand using a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your working arm fully extended overhead and your torso upright (a very slight lean back is optional), opposite hand resting on your thigh or the machine for stability.
Exhale and pull the handle straight down and slightly toward the side of your ribcage by driving your elbow down and back; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
At the bottom of the movement, pause and squeeze the lat and upper-back muscles, keeping the shoulder blade pulled down and back; avoid shrugging the shoulder up.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position with a controlled motion, allowing the lat to stretch but maintaining tension in the cable.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides. Avoid twisting the torso or using momentum — keep the movement controlled and driven by the lat.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed from a seated position to isolate each lat muscle while limiting trunk rotation. The exercise pulls a handle from overhead down toward the side of the chest, emphasizing a strong squeeze of the lats.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Pulldown (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads; sit upright with your chest against the chest pad if present.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads; sit upright with your chest against the chest pad if present.
Load the desired plates on the machine and grasp the overhead lever/bar with an overhand (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Start with your arms fully extended overhead, shoulders relaxed, and a slight natural arch in your spine — do not excessively lean back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the lever toward your upper chest; lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades as you reach the bottom of the motion and pause briefly while squeezing the lats.
Slowly return the lever to the start position under control until your arms are fully extended and the shoulders are not rounded forward.
Keep the torso still and avoid using momentum or excessive backward lean; breathe out while pulling down and inhale while returning to the start.
exercises.description: A seated, plate-loaded pulldown performed on a lever machine that targets the latissimus dorsi by pulling a fixed lever down from overhead toward the upper chest while keeping the torso stable. Emphasize elbow-driven movement and scapular retraction to maximize lat engagement.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Single Arm Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle on a high pulley and position an adjustable bench under it with the pad inclined ~30–45° so your chest will be supported when seated.
instructions: Set a single-handle on a high pulley and position an adjustable bench under it with the pad inclined ~30–45° so your chest will be supported when seated.
Sit on the bench facing the pulley, press your chest firmly against the incline pad and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability (chest-supported/prone on the incline pad).
Reach up with one arm and grasp the handle with a neutral grip (palm facing inward) so the arm is fully extended overhead — this is the start position.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbow down and back toward your hip, leading with the elbow and pulling the handle in an arc so the hand finishes beside your ribcage.
Squeeze the lat and retract the shoulder blade at the bottom of the movement, hold for a brief pause (1 second), then slowly allow the arm to extend back to the start under control.
Keep your chest pressed to the bench, avoid rotating or leaning the torso, and do not use momentum — exhale as you pull, inhale as you return to start.
Perform all repetitions on one side before switching arms to maintain consistent unilateral loading and focus on equal work per side.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed while chest-supported on an incline bench to isolate the latissimus dorsi. The high-pulley single-handle setup emphasizes unilateral pulling strength and minimizes torso cheat.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Stand upright in front of the plate-loaded lever machine with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up / supinated) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the dumbbells under your chest; brace your core and keep the neck in line with the spine.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows upward and backward, pulling the dumbbells toward the lower ribcage/upper waist while keeping elbows close to the body.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together without rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the weights up and inhale as you lower them; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. The lifter hip-hinges and pulls the dumbbells toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Lever Single Arm Reverse Grip High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up: load appropriate plate weight and adjust the seat/chest pad so the handle is within easy reach overhead when seated; sit with chest pressed against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set up: load appropriate plate weight and adjust the seat/chest pad so the handle is within easy reach overhead when seated; sit with chest pressed against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grip: reach up with one hand and take a reverse (supinated/palm-up) grip on the handle; keep the opposite hand resting on the machine or your knee for stability.
Starting position: sit upright with a slight natural arch in the low back, shoulder blades relaxed, and the working arm nearly extended (small bend in elbow).

Pull: exhale and pull the handle down and back in a controlled arc toward your upper chest/shoulder, leading with the elbow and driving the elbow toward your torso while squeezing the lats and upper-back (scapular retraction).
Hold and control: pause briefly (about 1 second) at the peak contraction while maintaining a stable torso; avoid rotating or leaning backward.
Return: inhale and slowly allow the handle to return to the start under control, extending the arm but not locking the elbow.
Reps and side switch: perform the desired number of repetitions with one arm, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain steady tempo and avoid using momentum or excessive shoulder shrugging.
exercises.description: A single-arm, supinated (reverse) grip high row on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes pulling the handle down and back from a high position to work the lats and upper-back musculature while stabilizing with the chest pad.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
Brace your core, keep the elbow straight (but not locked) and shoulders relaxed before initiating the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) the shoulder of the working arm straight up toward the ear, using only the shoulder girdle—do not roll or rotate the shoulder.
Pause and squeeze the traps at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on the contraction near the neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulder back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended and the trap is relaxed.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch arms; maintain neutral head position and avoid using momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm dumbbell shrug that elevates the shoulder to target the upper trapezius and surrounding neck muscles. Performed with one arm at a time to concentrate on trap contraction and improve unilateral strength and symmetry.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Stand upright in front of the plate-loaded lever machine with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand upright in front of the plate-loaded lever machine with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (thumbs-forward) grip so the loaded plates hang at arm's length; keep your arms straight and the torso tall with chest open.
Keep your head in a neutral position and core braced to prevent torso movement throughout the set.
Exhale and slowly shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the upper traps; avoid rolling the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds with a strong contraction in the traps.
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down in a controlled manner until arms are fully extended and traps are relaxed.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum or torso leaning.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs involve vertically elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles to isolate the trapezius muscles. The movement is a short, controlled shoulder elevation and lowering motion with the arms kept straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your chest pressed firmly against the chest pad, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your arms extended forward and a slight bend in the elbows, shoulders relaxed.
Exhale and pull the handles outward and back in a wide arc at shoulder height, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Stop when your arms are roughly in line with your torso or you feel a strong contraction in the rear delts and upper back; keep movement controlled and avoid shrugging.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend and chest against the pad.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping the torso stationary and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated reverse fly performed on a lever machine to strengthen the posterior delts and upper back by horizontally abducting the arms while seated with chest supported. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and rear shoulder activation.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable 45 degrees Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle on each high pulley and select a light-to-moderate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle on each high pulley and select a light-to-moderate weight. Stand facing the machine with the pulleys slightly above shoulder height.

Grab the left handle with your right hand and the right handle with your left hand (crossed grip) or hold each handle with the ipsilateral hand for a parallel setup; step back so there is tension in the cables.
Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, core braced and a slight hinge at the hips so your torso is nearly vertical to about 10–20°. Keep a neutral spine and gaze forward.
Start with your hands close together in front of you at chest height and elbows slightly bent (approx. 10–20°).

Pull both handles outward and back in a wide arc at roughly a 45° angle relative to your torso, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together at the end position so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping tension on the cables and avoiding upward shrugging of the shoulders.
Slowly and controlled, return the handles to the start position while maintaining the slight elbow bend and core tension.
Repeat for desired repetitions. Focus on using the posterior delts and upper back rather than momentum or heavy shoulder shrugging.
exercises.description: Standing cable 45° reverse fly performed with high pulleys; emphasizes the posterior delts and upper-upper back by abducting the arms in a rearward, slightly angled plane while retracting the scapulae. Use a controlled motion to isolate the rear delts and avoid using momentum.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.

Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.

Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.

exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Driver
exercises.notes: Move the dumbbell in a controlled circular motion around your head, keeping your core engaged.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell with both hands above your head.
Move the dumbbell in a controlled circular motion around your head, keeping your core engaged.
Complete the circle and return to the starting position, then repeat in the opposite direction.
exercises.description: The Dumbbell Standing Driver targets the shoulder muscles by moving the dumbbell in a circular motion around the head. This exercise helps to strengthen and tone the delts.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine weight so you can perform controlled reps without shoulder shrugging.
instructions: Adjust the machine weight so you can perform controlled reps without shoulder shrugging.
Stand upright beside the lever with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; grasp the handle with the working hand (thumb wrapped) at your side.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and the wrist neutral; maintain an upright torso with shoulders down and back (no leaning or twisting).
Exhale and lift the arm straight out to the side in a smooth arc until the upper arm reaches roughly shoulder height (parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the scapula stable and avoiding excessive shoulder elevation.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position under control, resisting any momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides if performing single-arm sets.
exercises.description: A standing lateral raise performed on a lever/cable station that isolates the lateral head of the deltoid by lifting the arm out to the side from the body. The movement uses a controlled upward arc to shoulder height and a slow descent to emphasize the delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Y Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward (~30–45°), keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a flat, braced spine (standing bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a light dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward (~30–45°), keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a flat, braced spine (standing bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the weights close to your thighs and shoulders relaxed away from your ears.
Keeping a small bend in the elbows and thumbs pointing up, raise the dumbbells up and out at about a 45° angle from the body to form a Y shape (lead with the thumbs).
Focus on scapular control—initiate the movement from the delts/upper back, squeeze the shoulder blades gently at the top without overarching the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for desired reps, using light weight to maintain strict form and avoid momentum or excessive neck/upper-trap shrugging.
exercises.description: Standing bent-over Y raise is a hip-hinged shoulder exercise that targets the delts by lifting light dumbbells in a Y-shape overhead while stabilizing the scapula. It emphasizes posterior/upper shoulder and upper back activation for improved posture and scapular control.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.

The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.

Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar).
instructions: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar). Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip and bring your elbows to your sides so your upper arms are vertical and stationary.
Exhale and press the rope down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the rope ends are slightly spread apart at the bottom.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping shoulders down and upper arms fixed; avoid using torso swing or stepping for momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the cable to raise your hands back to the start position, controlling the eccentric phase until your forearms return to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining tucked elbows and a stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdowns (rope attachment) target the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance while the upper arms remain stationary. The movement also engages the forearms for grip and the delts and abs for posture and stabilization.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Curl
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with a supinated (palm-up) grip using one hand.
Start with the working arm fully extended at your side and the elbow close to your torso; brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine.
Keeping the upper arm stationary and the elbow fixed at your side, curl the handle upward by contracting the biceps until your forearm is near vertical or the knuckles approach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the biceps, then slowly lower the handle back to the start position under control to emphasize the eccentric phase.
Avoid swinging the torso or using the shoulder and delts to lift; if you need momentum, reduce the weight.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat with the opposite arm, maintaining the same posture and tempo for both sides.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm curl using a low cable pulley to load the biceps through the concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering; performed one arm at a time to emphasize unilateral strength and minimize momentum. Keep the elbow tucked to isolate the biceps and use a full range of motion.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with one hand in a neutral grip.
Tuck the working elbow to your side so the upper arm is stationary and bent about 90°; brace your core and adopt a slight forward lean from the hips if needed for balance.
Exhale and press the handle down by extending the elbow until the arm is fully straight and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, focusing on squeezing the triceps and avoiding shoulder or upper-arm movement.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the elbow is about 90°; do not let the upper arm drift away from your side.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch to the other arm. Avoid using body momentum or swinging to move the weight.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against the cable's resistance while the upper arm remains stationary at the side. Performed standing with a single handle attached to a high pulley for constant tension.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Drag Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged (standing position).
Grasp an EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and let the bar hang against your thighs.
Start with your elbows slightly behind the torso and the bar touching or very close to the upper thighs.
Keeping the bar close to your body, drive your elbows backward (toward your hips) and pull the bar straight up along your torso — do not flare the elbows forward.
Curl the bar until it reaches around chest/upper abdomen level and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the bar back down along the same path until your arms are fully extended and the bar returns to the thighs, maintaining elbow position behind the body.
Breathe out while curling up and inhale while lowering; avoid swinging the torso or using momentum and keep movement controlled.
Perform desired reps while keeping wrists neutral and ensuring the bar remains in contact with or very close to the body throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing EZ-bar drag curl where the bar is pulled up close to the body while the elbows travel back, emphasizing the biceps peak and reducing shoulder involvement compared to a standard curl.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Single Arm Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core engaged to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core engaged to maintain a neutral spine.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand and press it overhead until your arm is fully extended; keep the wrist neutral and elbow pointing forward/up.
Optionally place your free hand on the working upper arm or elbow for additional support and to help keep the upper arm vertical.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell by flexing the elbow, bringing the weight down behind your head until your forearm is roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Keep the upper arm stationary and avoid excessive leaning or flaring of the elbow; only the forearm should move.
Exhale and press the dumbbell back up by extending the elbow until the arm is straight but not locked out aggressively.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Use a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or excessive trunk movement.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm overhead triceps extension isolates the triceps by extending the elbow with a dumbbell held overhead while the upper arm remains stationary. It is performed one arm at a time to address unilateral strength and imbalance.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (upright torso).
Grab the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing down or slightly toward you), hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the bar at chest/upper-ab level, elbows tucked close to your sides and upper arms stationary (forearms up).
Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging or leaning forward.
Pause briefly at full extension, then inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows while maintaining upper-arm position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping movement controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar that emphasizes elbow extension to work the triceps; performed facing the cable machine with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Keep a neutral spine and controlled tempo, extending the arms fully and returning under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

exercises.name: Cable High Pulley Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set a rope or handle on a high pulley.
instructions: Set a rope or handle on a high pulley. Stand facing away from the machine with feet in a staggered stance (one foot forward, one back) and grip the rope with both hands overhead.
Lean forward slightly at the hips, brace your core (abs engaged), and keep your upper arms fixed close to your head so the elbows point forward/up throughout the set.
Begin with your forearms bent so the rope is behind your head; this is the start position. Keep your delts and torso stable — only the elbows should move.
Exhale and extend your elbows, pressing the rope forward and up until your arms are nearly straight while maintaining a small, controlled bend in the elbows at lockout.
Pause briefly at full extension to maximize triceps contraction, then inhale and slowly allow the rope to return to the start position behind your head under control.
Repeat for the desired reps. Maintain neutral wrist alignment, avoid flaring the elbows outward, and keep the core tight to prevent arching the lower back.
exercises.description: An overhead triceps extension performed with a high pulley and rope attachment to load the long head of the triceps; executed from a slightly forward-leaning, staggered stance to increase stability. The movement isolates elbow extension while the torso and shoulders stabilize the load.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
instructions: Sit on a preacher (angled) curl bench with feet flat on the floor and chest/abdomen lightly against the pad so your upper arms rest flat on the sloped pad; body should be stable and upright.
Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above the pad; this is the start position (elbows slightly bent but not locked).

Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder movement; focus on contracting the biceps as the bar approaches chest level.
Pause and squeeze the biceps briefly at the top of the movement without lifting the elbows off the pad or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are near full extension, maintaining contact between upper arms and pad throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows strict form; avoid using momentum or arching the back to lift the bar.
If wrists or forearms fatigue prematurely, reduce weight or shorten range slightly to maintain strict elbow-driven motion and protect joints.
exercises.description: Seated preacher curl using an EZ bar to isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arms on an angled bench pad; emphasizes controlled elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement. The angled pad reduces cheating and increases time under tension for the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: EZ barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench and load an EZ bar with appropriate weight; position a spotter if using heavy loads.
instructions: Set up a flat bench and load an EZ bar with appropriate weight; position a spotter if using heavy loads.
Lie supine on the bench with your eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor and knees bent to create a stable base.
Grasp the EZ bar with a narrow/close grip (hands placed inside the outer bends of the EZ bar), wrists straight and palms facing up/neutral.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly over your chest with arms extended, shoulders retracted slightly and core braced.
Slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the middle of your chest/nipple line while keeping your elbows close to your torso (not flared wide).
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then drive the bar upward by extending the elbows until your arms are nearly straight but not fully hyperextended.
Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower. Maintain wrist alignment and a tight torso throughout the set.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, and carefully rack the bar with control at the end of the set.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a flat bench, perform a close-grip press with an EZ bar to emphasize elbow extension and overload the triceps while still engaging the chest and front delts. Use a narrow grip and keep the elbows tucked to maximize triceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Wide Grip Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees slightly soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees slightly soft and core braced.
Load an EZ-bar or barbell and take a wide, underhand (palms-up) grip on the outer angled or outer knurling so your hands are wider than shoulder width.
Let the bar hang at arm's length in front of your thighs with elbows by your sides and wrists neutral.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary and avoiding shoulder drive or torso lean.
Continue the curl until your forearms are near your biceps and you feel a full contraction; pause briefly and squeeze the biceps.
Inhale and lower the bar in a slow, controlled motion back to full elbow extension while maintaining upright posture.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Avoid swinging the body or excessively moving the elbows forward.
exercises.description: Standing wide-grip curls performed with an EZ/barbell target the biceps by elbow flexion while the forearms and delts assist with grip and stabilization. Using a wider hand placement emphasizes a slightly different biceps activation and limits ROM compared with a narrow grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Stand facing the preacher pad with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
instructions: Stand facing the preacher pad with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
Place your chest/upper arms on the pad so your armpits align with the top edge and your elbows rest comfortably on the pad.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a supinated (palms up) grip and let your arms hang straight on the pad (starting position).
Keeping your upper arms pressed to the pad and shoulders still, curl the dumbbells by flexing the elbows until your forearms approach your biceps.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement without leaning back or using momentum.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the fully extended position on the pad with controlled tension, resisting the weight throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining strict form and avoiding shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: A variation of the preacher curl performed standing with dumbbells while the upper arms are supported on a preacher pad to isolate the biceps. The movement emphasizes elbow flexion while minimizing shoulder movement.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Reverse Grip Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees soft and core braced.
Grip an EZ bar with an overhand (reverse) grip so palms face down; use a shoulder-width or slightly narrower hand position on the angled bar.
Let the bar hang at thigh level with arms fully extended and elbows close to your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the bar up by flexing the elbows until the bar reaches just below chest/upper abdomen.
Pause briefly at the top with wrists neutral but palms still facing down, focusing on contracting the forearms and biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with controlled motion until arms are fully extended.
Avoid swinging the torso or using shoulder elevation; keep elbows fixed at your sides and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Use a weight that allows strict form and full range of motion; reduce load if you must cheat with momentum.
exercises.description: Standing reverse-grip curl using an EZ bar where palms face down; this emphasizes the forearm extensors/brachioradialis and also works the biceps as a secondary mover. Performed with strict elbow flexion and minimal body swing.

exercises.name: Barbell Drag Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a barbell with a shoulder-width, supinated (palms-up) grip and arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a barbell with a shoulder-width, supinated (palms-up) grip and arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back and down, and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Begin the rep by driving your elbows backward (toward your hips) while simultaneously pulling the bar straight up the front of your body so the bar stays in contact with or very close to your torso.
Focus on leading the movement with the elbows rather than swinging the wrists; the forearms should move upward as the elbows travel back so the bar drags along the body.
Continue until your forearms are vertical or the bar reaches chest/upper-abdomen level and you feel a full biceps contraction; briefly squeeze at the top.
Lower the bar under control along the same path, allowing the elbows to move forward until the arms are fully extended, keeping the bar close to the body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while avoiding torso swing, shoulder elevation, or using momentum to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A standing curl variation where the bar is pulled up close to the body while the elbows move back (dragging the bar along the torso) to emphasize the biceps peak and long head. The movement reduces shoulder flexion and encourages a more vertical forearm path.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with one hand in a neutral grip.
Tuck the working elbow to your side so the upper arm is stationary and bent about 90°; brace your core and adopt a slight forward lean from the hips if needed for balance.
Exhale and press the handle down by extending the elbow until the arm is fully straight and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, focusing on squeezing the triceps and avoiding shoulder or upper-arm movement.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the elbow is about 90°; do not let the upper arm drift away from your side.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch to the other arm. Avoid using body momentum or swinging to move the weight.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against the cable's resistance while the upper arm remains stationary at the side. Performed standing with a single handle attached to a high pulley for constant tension.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Reverse Grip Curl (Straight bar)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart and core braced.
Grasp the bar with a pronated (palms-down) grip about shoulder-width apart, arms fully extended and elbows close to your sides.
Keep your torso still and shoulders down; this is a standing position throughout the set.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, focusing the effort into the forearms and biceps; avoid swinging the torso or lifting the elbows forward.
Pause and squeeze briefly at the top of the movement when forearms/biceps are fully contracted.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start under control until arms are nearly fully extended, maintaining tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding wrist collapse or excessive shoulder involvement.
exercises.description: Standing reverse-grip cable curl is a pronated (palms-down) curling movement performed with a straight bar on a low pulley that emphasizes the forearm muscles (brachioradialis) while also working the biceps. It uses a controlled elbow flexion movement against constant cable tension.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
