instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable Rope Seated Row
exercises.notes: Grasp the rope attachment with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and sit upright with a neutral spine and chest up; start with arms fully extended.
instructions: Sit on a seated row bench or platform with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the rope attachment with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and sit upright with a neutral spine and chest up; start with arms fully extended.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction) and drive your elbows straight back toward your torso.
Pull the rope toward your upper abdomen or lower chest, keeping elbows close to the body and leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at peak contraction while squeezing the lats and upper back, maintaining an upright torso (avoid excessive trunk lean or momentum).
Slowly extend the arms back to the starting position with control, returning the shoulders to a slightly protracted position while keeping the spine neutral.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, ensuring wrists remain neutral and movements are smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated cable rope row performed from a bench using a rope attachment to pull the handle toward the torso, emphasizing scapular retraction and elbow drive to work the lats and upper back. Keeps a neutral spine and controlled tempo to isolate the pulling muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley.
instructions: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley. Adjust a bench or chest pad so your chest is firmly supported and you can sit upright without leaning forward or rotating.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest pressed into the support, and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one arm; start with the arm extended and shoulder slightly protracted.
Brace your core, keep your torso stationary against the chest support, and retract your shoulder blade slightly before initiating the pull.
Pull the handle down and back in a smooth arc by driving your elbow up and back toward your ribcage, focusing on squeezing the upper back and rear delt at the end range.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while maintaining a neutral wrist and no torso rotation or shrugging, then slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the return) and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm high cable row performed with chest support to isolate the upper back while minimizing torso movement. The movement pulls a high-set handle down and back with the elbow driving toward the ribcage to emphasize the upper back and rear delts.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Lever One Arm Lateral High Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and chest pad so the handle is reachable overhead when seated and your chest is supported against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and chest pad so the handle is reachable overhead when seated and your chest is supported against the pad.
Select an appropriate weight and sit with feet flat on the floor, chest braced against the pad, and torso upright (no leaning or twisting).
Reach up with one hand and grasp the high lateral handle with a neutral or overhand grip; keep the opposite hand resting or holding a support if available.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blade and pulling the handle down and slightly back in a controlled arc toward the side of your upper ribcage, keeping the elbow high and out to emphasize the upper back and rear delts.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak contraction for 1–2 seconds while maintaining a stable, upright torso (avoid rotating or jerking).
Slowly reverse the motion to return the handle to the start under control, fully extending the arm without collapsing the shoulder.
Perform the desired number of reps for one side, then switch arms and repeat, keeping form consistent on both sides.
exercises.description: Seated one-arm lateral high row performed on a lever machine; you pull a high, lateral handle down and back in an arc to target the upper back and rear shoulder area. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and the posterior shoulder while using the arm muscles to assist.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.
instructions: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower, hands directly in front of hips and the bar resting against the thighs (standing position).
Keeping the torso vertical, initiate the movement by driving the elbows up and out while pulling the bar close to the body.
Continue lifting until the bar reaches approximately collarbone/upper-chest level or until elbows are roughly shoulder height; lead the movement with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and chest tall, avoiding excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, fully extending the arms but maintaining tension in the delts and traps.
Breathe out while lifting and inhale while lowering. Use a controlled range — avoid lifting the elbows higher than shoulder level to reduce shoulder impingement risk.
exercises.description: A standing barbell upright row that primarily targets the delts by elevating the elbows and pulling the bar toward the upper chest, with significant assistance from the traps and the arm muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Stance Single Arm Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Assume a split stance: place one foot forward and the other back with the back foot on the toes; knees should be slightly bent and weight distributed mainly on the front heel for stability.
instructions: Assume a split stance: place one foot forward and the other back with the back foot on the toes; knees should be slightly bent and weight distributed mainly on the front heel for stability.
Pick up a dumbbell with the working hand and bring it to the rack position at shoulder height (dumbbell beside the shoulder, elbow tucked, palm facing inward or slightly forward).
Brace your abs and squeeze the glutes to create a stable, upright torso; keep hips squared and avoid rotating toward the pressing side.
Press the dumbbell straight up in a controlled path until the arm is fully extended overhead, exhaling as you press; keep the wrist neutral and the elbow close to the plane of the body.
Pause briefly at the top with the weight over or slightly behind the shoulder line (not forward of the head) and avoid overarching the lower back.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the rack position under control while inhaling, maintaining core tension and the split stance balance.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch legs and repeat on the other side.
exercises.description: A unilateral overhead press performed from a split stance that primarily works the delts while forcing core and lower-body stabilization. The single-arm demand increases shoulder stability and recruits triceps for lockout and leg/glute muscles to maintain balance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.

exercises.name: Cable Upright Row
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar or close-grip handle; stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar or close-grip handle; stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip (palms facing toward your body) and let your arms hang straight down in front of your thighs; keep a slight bend in the knees.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and spine neutral, and pull the handle straight up by driving your elbows out and up (elbows should stay higher than your wrists).
Raise the handle until your elbows reach about shoulder height or the bar nears your upper chest/collarbone, focusing on contracting the delts and traps.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and neutral wrists; avoid excessive neck or torso lean and avoid pulling the bar above shoulder level.
Slowly lower the handle back to the start under control until your arms are fully extended, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady breathing: exhale on the pull, inhale on the lowering phase.
Use a moderate weight and controlled tempo to avoid using momentum; if you feel shoulder impingement or excessive shrugging, reduce range of motion or weight.
exercises.description: A standing cable upright row where you pull a low-pulley handle upward toward the upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (delts) muscles while also engaging the traps and arms. Performed upright with elbows leading the movement and wrists neutral.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Standing Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand tall (upright) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold an EZ bar in front of your thighs with a pronated (overhand) narrow to shoulder-width grip.
instructions: Stand tall (upright) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold an EZ bar in front of your thighs with a pronated (overhand) narrow to shoulder-width grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back, and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the bar close to the body and leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Raise the bar to approximately upper chest / lower clavicle height (or until elbows reach around shoulder level), keeping wrists relatively neutral to avoid excessive bending.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but without hyperextending or shrugging excessively.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms but maintaining tension in the shoulders.
Breathe out as you lift the bar and inhale as you lower it; avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean to move the weight.
Use a lighter weight and a slightly wider grip if you experience shoulder impingement; stop if you feel sharp shoulder pain.
exercises.description: A standing upright row performed with an EZ bar that elevates the bar toward the upper chest, emphasizing the lateral delts and upper traps while keeping the torso upright. Use a close grip and lead with the elbows to maximize shoulder activation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.

exercises.name: Lever Seated One Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Angled Leg Press
exercises.notes: Disengage the safety/locks while keeping control of the sled with your hands on the handles.
instructions: Position yourself supine on the angled leg press sled with your back and head fully supported against the pad; grip the side handles for stability.

Place your feet about shoulder-width on the platform (mid-foot placement); toes slightly pointed up so heels remain contact points. Adjust feet higher on the platform to emphasize glutes/hamstrings or lower to emphasize quads.
Disengage the safety/locks while keeping control of the sled with your hands on the handles.
Start with knees bent to roughly 90 degrees (or a comfortable depth), feet flat on the platform and core braced to keep the lower back pressed into the pad.
Exhale and press the platform away by extending the knees, driving through the heels and mid-foot until legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the legs, then inhale and slowly lower the platform by bending the knees under control back toward the starting position.
Keep knees tracking over your toes and prevent them from caving inward; maintain a neutral spine and avoid lifting your hips off the pad.
After completing the set, re-engage the safety/locks before releasing your grip and exiting the sled.
exercises.description: A supine, angled sled leg press that primarily targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and calves. Performed by pressing the platform away with the feet while the back remains supported on the angled seat.

exercises.name: Landmine Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
Stand facing the bar with a wide (sumo) stance: feet wider than shoulder-width, toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed through the heels and midfoot.
Hold the bar close to your body at chest level with elbows down and a neutral spine; brace your core (abs) and keep chest tall and head neutral.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, letting them track outward over your toes; descend until your thighs are about parallel to the floor or a comfortable depth with no lumbar rounding.
Keep the bar close, maintain an upright torso, and feel tension in the glutes and adductors throughout the descent.
Drive back up by pressing through the heels and pushing your knees out, extending the hips and knees to stand while maintaining core bracing and chest position.
Control the bar on the way down for each rep; exhale as you drive up and inhale as you descend.
Maintain knee alignment (tracking toes), neutral spine, and avoid collapsing the knees inward or letting the torso fold forward.
exercises.description: A wide-stance squat performed with a landmine bar held in front of the body; the sumo stance shifts emphasis to the glutes and inner thighs while the landmine setup helps maintain an upright torso. It builds hip extension strength and targets adductors and quads as you drive up from the bottom position.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Step onto a raised, stable platform or aerobic step so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can drop freely; stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width and toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a comfortable overhand grip to maintain control; engage your abs and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout (standing position).
Keep knees straight but not locked (a slight soft bend) and maintain weight balanced over the balls of your feet.
Exhale and raise your heels by plantarflexing at the ankle, lifting as high as possible onto your toes and pausing 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent — do not drop quickly or bounce.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and full range of motion. Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack if using heavy weight.
When finished, carefully step down from the platform and re-rack the bar in the squat rack.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed from a raised platform with a barbell across the upper back; rise onto the toes to concentrically contract the calves, then lower the heels below the platform for a full stretch. This is a standing, weight-bearing isolation exercise for the calves with upper-back and core stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Seated One Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.

Sit upright with your back pressed firmly against the back pad, chest up, and grasp the handles or seat grips for stability; position the non-working foot flat on the floor or on the machine footrest.
Start with the working leg extended (knee nearly straight) and the ankle neutral; this is the starting position.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knee, driving the heel toward the underside of your glute while keeping the thigh and hips stationary against the seat.
Pause briefly at the top of the contraction and squeeze the hamstrings; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips.
Inhale and slowly return the lower leg to the starting extended position under control, resisting the weight on the way down.
Complete all reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg. Maintain a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Seated single‑leg curl performed on a lever machine to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to correct imbalances and increase muscle activation.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean grip Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set up the barbell in a front-rack position using a clean grip: hands just outside shoulders, fingers under the bar, elbows lifted so upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor; stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Set up the barbell in a front-rack position using a clean grip: hands just outside shoulders, fingers under the bar, elbows lifted so upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor; stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core and keep chest up and back straight (neutral spine); eyes forward and shoulders high to support the bar on the front deltoids/upper chest.
Inhale, initiate the movement by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, pushing the knees outward to track over the toes and keeping the elbows up so the bar does not roll forward.
Descend under control until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth while maintaining an upright torso), keeping weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining core tension and a proud chest, then drive the feet into the ground (focus on heels) to extend the knees and hips and return to standing.
Exhale as you stand up, fully extending hips and knees while keeping elbows high and the bar stable on your shoulders; reset breathing and posture before the next rep.

Keep repetitions controlled and avoid letting the torso collapse forward, the elbows drop, or the knees cave inward; if wrist discomfort occurs with the clean grip, adjust hand position or use cross-arm/front-rack variation.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat performed with a clean grip (bar rests on the front shoulders, elbows high) that emphasizes knee extension to target the quadriceps while also engaging the posterior chain and core for stability. Maintain an upright torso and high elbows throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, dumbbells held in front of thighs with a neutral grip; keep a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff‑leg position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, dumbbells held in front of thighs with a neutral grip; keep a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff‑leg position).
Brace your core and retract the shoulders slightly to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them straight back while keeping the dumbbells close to your legs; allow the torso to come forward until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (usually to mid‑shin).
Keep the back flat (avoid rounding) and maintain the slight knee bend; eyes should be looking a few feet ahead to help keep the spine neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom when you feel a firm hamstring stretch, then drive the hips forward by contracting the hamstrings and glutes to return to standing.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and avoid leaning back or hyperextending the spine.
Breathe in while lowering, exhale as you drive the hips forward; maintain controlled tempo and a firm grip (forearms engaged) throughout.
exercises.description: A hinge-style deadlift performed with minimal knee bend to load the posterior chain, primarily targeting the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back. Dumbbells are lowered along the legs by driving the hips back and then the hips are thrust forward to return to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
instructions: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
Position your feet much wider than shoulder-width with toes pointed out at ~30–45 degrees (sumo stance).
Brace your core, keep chest tall and a neutral spine, and allow a slight forward torso lean while maintaining an upright upper body.

Begin the movement by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, lowering yourself into a squat until your thighs are roughly parallel to the platform or a comfortable depth where knees track over toes.
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and distribute weight through your heels and mid-foot; avoid collapsing the knees inward.
Drive forcefully through the heels and hips to return to standing, fully extending the hips at the top while resisting the lever’s load throughout the motion.
Control the descent on each rep, breathe in on the way down and exhale as you drive up, and perform repetitions with a steady tempo.
exercises.description: A wide-stance (sumo) squat performed with a lever belt/harness attached to a machine; emphasizes hip drive and inner-thigh engagement while reducing load on the spine. Use a wide, turned-out foot position and sit back between the heels, then drive up through the hips.

exercises.name: Barbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Position a loaded barbell in a rack at upper‑chest height, step under it and place it across your upper back/traps, hands slightly wider than shoulder width with a firm overhand grip; unrack the bar and take one or two steps back to a stable, shoulder‑width starting stance (standing).
instructions: Position a loaded barbell in a rack at upper‑chest height, step under it and place it across your upper back/traps, hands slightly wider than shoulder width with a firm overhand grip; unrack the bar and take one or two steps back to a stable, shoulder‑width starting stance (standing).
Brace your abs and keep a neutral spine and chest up; feet should be hip to shoulder width for balance and toes pointing forward.
Take a controlled long step forward with one foot (forward lunge), planting the front foot flat while keeping weight centered over the midfoot to heel.

Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the rear knee is close to the ground (approximately 90° at the front knee); keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid excessive forward collapse of the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip and return to the starting standing position.
Repeat for the desired reps, alternating legs each repetition or complete all reps on one side before switching; breathe in while lowering and exhale while driving up.
Maintain controlled tempo, core bracing, and even foot placement each rep; use a power rack or spotter for safety when loading heavy.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed with a barbell resting on the upper back that emphasizes single‑leg knee extension to work the front thigh while also engaging the hips and core for stability. Each repetition involves stepping forward, lowering until the front thigh is near parallel to the floor, then driving back to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position).
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position).
Select an appropriate weight and ensure the machine's safety catch or lever is engaged before sitting.
Place the balls of both feet shoulder-width on the footplate with toes pointing forward; heels should hang slightly to allow a full range of motion.
Grip the side handles or seat handles to stabilize the torso and unlock the safety if applicable.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet, driving the footplate away by extending your ankles into full plantarflexion (raise your heels as high as possible).
Pause briefly at the top for a strong contraction in the calves, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves (controlled eccentric phase).
Repeat for desired reps. After finishing, lock the safety catch before removing your feet from the footplate.
exercises.description: Seated calf press performed on a lever machine targets the calf muscles by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while seated with knees bent. It isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus complex for focused calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
instructions: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
Grip the side handles and ensure your lower back and hips are pressed firmly against the backpad; unlock the sled safety/locks if present.
Start position: Bend your knees to bring your thighs toward your chest until knees are about 90 degrees, keeping feet flat on the plate and knees tracking over toes.
Press: Exhale and drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend your knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but do not lock the knees at full extension.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the sled back down by bending the knees, lowering the weight under tension until your knees reach about 90 degrees again.
Repetitions: Repeat for the desired number of reps with controlled tempo; avoid bouncing at the bottom or using momentum to lock the knees.
Safety cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout, avoid lifting hips off the pad, and keep knees aligned with feet. Rerack/lock the sled before exiting.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed supine on an inclined sled platform where you push the footplate away using the legs. The movement primarily extends the knees to target the quadriceps while the glutes, hamstrings, and calves assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Barbell Narrow Stance Squat
exercises.notes: Setup: Rack the barbell at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Setup: Rack the barbell at about mid-chest height. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back on the posterior delts (not on the neck). Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack and stance: Unrack the bar and take one or two small steps back. Stand with a narrow stance — feet close together or hip-width at most, toes pointing forward or slightly outward.
Brace core and posture: Keep a neutral spine, chest up, shoulders back, and brace your abs. Eyes forward to maintain alignment.

Descent: Inhale and begin the squat by bending at the hips and knees, sending your hips back slightly while keeping the knees tracking over the toes. Lower until your thighs are about parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows).
Control and alignment: Avoid letting the knees collapse inward; press them slightly outward in line with your toes. Keep weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels, not the toes.
Ascent: Exhale and drive upward through the heels, extending the knees and hips to return to the standing position while maintaining a tight core and neutral spine.
Finish and rack: Reset posture at the top, then carefully step forward and rack the bar when the set is complete.
Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins when loading heavy. Keep repetitions controlled and prioritize form over depth or load to protect knees and lower back.
exercises.description: A barbell squat performed with a narrow foot stance that places greater emphasis on the quadriceps while still engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core. The lifter holds a barbell across the upper back and lowers hips down and back before returning to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Dimel Deadlift
exercises.notes: Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Rear Kick
exercises.notes: Set the lever or ankle attachment to the appropriate height and select a manageable resistance; stand facing the machine and grip the front handles for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Raise Crunch (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your lower back is supported and the roller pad sits just above your ankles when knees are bent.

workout.title: Leg Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Leg Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height. Step under the bar and place it across the rear delts/upper trapezius area just below the top of the shoulders (low-bar position), not on the neck.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, retract the shoulder blades and squeeze the upper back to create a stable shelf; elbows should be pointed slightly down and back to support the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening the legs and take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack. Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core (tighten abs and breathe into the belly) and maintain a neutral spine; keep chest up and eyes forward. The torso will lean forward more than a high-bar squat but should remain rigid.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously; keep the knees tracking over the toes and push them outward to avoid caving in.
Lower until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to your comfortable depth while maintaining form), keeping weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Drive upward by pushing through the heels and extending the hips and knees simultaneously while maintaining a braced core and tight upper back; exhale as you rise.
Return to a fully upright standing position with hips locked out, then repeat for the desired reps. Control the bar on each rep and reset your breath and brace between reps if needed.
When finished, walk the bar forward and carefully re-rack it on the squat rack. Use a spotter or safety pins if lifting heavy or training near failure.
exercises.description: A loaded barbell squat with the bar positioned low on the rear shoulders (across the posterior deltoids), emphasizing the thighs and posterior chain. The movement consists of a controlled hip-back-and-down descent to roughly parallel followed by an explosive drive back to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Dimel Deadlift
exercises.notes: Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar with both hands just outside the legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips back (hip hinge) and lower the barbell down the front of your legs toward mid-shin while keeping the bar close to your body.
Keep the neck neutral, shoulders retracted, and do not let the lower back round as you descend; feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward explosively while pulling the bar up along the thighs to a near-lockout (stand tall), squeezing the glutes at the top.
Control the descent and repeat for the desired reps, breathing in on the lower and exhaling as you drive the hips forward.
Use lighter weight and higher reps if performing Dimel-style sets, and stop or reduce range if you feel lower-back rounding or pain.
exercises.description: A barbell hip-hinge exercise performed from a standing position that emphasizes eccentric and concentric work of the hamstrings by lowering the bar toward the shins and driving the hips forward to stand. The movement trains the posterior chain with a short range of motion and controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Rear Kick
exercises.notes: Set the lever or ankle attachment to the appropriate height and select a manageable resistance; stand facing the machine and grip the front handles for stability.
instructions: Set the lever or ankle attachment to the appropriate height and select a manageable resistance; stand facing the machine and grip the front handles for stability.

Position the working leg so the cuff or pad is secured at the back of the thigh or ankle (depending on machine); stand upright on the support leg with a slight bend in the knee and feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs engaged), keep the torso upright with a small forward lean if needed for balance, and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive the working leg straight back by extending at the hip (avoid excessive lumbar arching), squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement; keep the knee slightly bent and the foot neutral.
Pause briefly at full hip extension, then return the leg under control to the starting position without letting the weight stack slam.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, then switch sides. Breathe out on the kickback (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
exercises.description: A standing hip extension performed on a lever/cable machine where one leg drives backward against resistance to isolate and strengthen the glute muscles. The exercise is executed while standing and holding the machine for balance, focusing on controlled hip extension and glute contraction.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
Sit with your back fully supported against the pad and both feet placed hip-width on the lower edge of the foot platform (balls of feet and toes on the platform, heels free to move).
Grip the handles or seat sides for stability and ensure the safety latch or stops are set before starting.
Unlatch the machine (if applicable) and begin from a neutral/dorsiflexed position with a slight stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet by plantarflexing the ankles to raise the platform until your calves are fully contracted; do not use the knees or hips to drive the movement.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, then inhale and slowly lower the platform under control until you feel a stretch in the calves (avoid dropping quickly or bouncing).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then re-engage the safety latch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever calf press machine, this exercise uses controlled ankle plantarflexion to raise the foot platform and load the calf muscles. It emphasizes the gastrocnemius/soleus complex while the torso and knees remain supported.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Raise Crunch (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your lower back is supported and the roller pad sits just above your ankles when knees are bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your lower back is supported and the roller pad sits just above your ankles when knees are bent.
Load the desired plate weight and sit with your back against the back pad, knees bent and feet secured under the roller pad; grasp the machine handles near your head to stabilize the upper body.
Start with torso upright and knees pulled slightly toward the torso (neutral tension) and inhale.
Exhale and simultaneously curl your spine forward and lift your knees slightly toward your chest by contracting your abs, performing a controlled crunch + leg raise against the plate resistance.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the contraction in your abs and avoiding excessive neck strain or momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, extending the spine and lowering the knees under control until tension is maintained.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, keeping movements slow and controlled and avoiding excessive lumbar rounding or swinging.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded seated crunch that combines a leg raise with a spinal crunch to emphasize the rectus abdominis. Performed seated with feet hooked under a roller pad while curling the torso and hips forward against resistance.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Bend
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; hold one dumbbell in the hand on the working side with the arm extended down by your thigh.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; hold one dumbbell in the hand on the working side with the arm extended down by your thigh.
Place the non-working hand lightly behind your head or across your chest to avoid pulling on the neck; keep your chest up and shoulders back (standing position).
Keep your hips and pelvis fixed — do not shift them laterally — and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Slowly bend your torso directly to the side of the dumbbell (lateral flexion) by contracting the obliques; lead with the ribcage so the movement comes from the torso, not the hips.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the range without rotating or leaning forward/backward, then contract the abs to return the torso to the starting upright position in a controlled manner.
Breathe in as you lower the torso and exhale as you return upright; perform the desired number of reps then switch sides.
Use a weight that allows smooth, controlled repetitions and avoid jerking or using momentum to pull the torso — stop if you feel low-back pain.
exercises.description: Standing lateral trunk flexion performed while holding a dumbbell at one side to load the obliques. The movement bends the torso toward the weight and then returns upright to work the abs (obliques) with grip and spinal stabilization assistance.

exercises.name: Cable Twist
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a single handle to a cable pulley positioned at about chest height.
instructions: Setup: Attach a single handle to a cable pulley positioned at about chest height. Stand upright facing the pulley with feet shoulder-width apart or in a slightly staggered stance for balance.
Grip & posture: Grasp the handle with both hands out in front of you, arms extended but not locked, shoulders down and back, knees slightly bent, and spine neutral.
Start position: Take a small step away from the machine so the cable is taut and the weight stack is lifted slightly; keep hips facing forward (minimize hip rotation).
Execute rotation: Exhale and rotate your torso away from the machine by driving the ribcage and shoulders across your hips, keeping your arms extended and the handle at roughly chest height.
Control the return: Inhale and slowly return to the start position under control, resisting the pull of the cable. Do not let the cable snap you back.
Repetitions & tempo: Perform the desired number of controlled repetitions on one side, then switch sides by repositioning so you rotate in the opposite direction.
Form tips: Keep the movement driven by the core (abs) not by the arms; maintain a neutral spine and avoid excessive lumbar extension or leaning. Adjust foot stance for stability as needed.
exercises.description: A standing cable rotation where you hold a single handle with both hands and rotate the torso away from the machine, using resisted trunk rotation to train the abs and improve core rotational strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
