Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Lever Low Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the desired plates on the machine and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended forward at the start position.
instructions: Sit on the seat with your chest pressed against the pad (chest-supported), feet flat on the footrests or floor and knees slightly bent.
Load the desired plates on the machine and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended forward at the start position.
Pull your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction) to initiate the movement, then drive your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Continue pulling until the handles reach your torso or the elbows are behind the body, squeezing the lats and upper back at the top for a 1-2 second pause.
Slowly return the handles to the start position under control by extending the arms and allowing the shoulder blades to protract, keeping tension on the lats throughout.
Keep your torso stable and chest on the pad the entire set — avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum to complete reps.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric); choose a weight that allows full range of motion with good form.
exercises.description: A seated, plate-loaded low row variation that targets the latissimus dorsi with emphasis on mid-back scapular retraction; performed from a chest-supported seated position using the machine's lever handles. Focuses on a controlled horizontal pulling motion to develop back thickness and arm pulling strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench to a decline angle and place your feet securely under the foot pads so you are stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set the bench to a decline angle and place your feet securely under the foot pads so you are stable and supine on the bench.

Pick up two dumbbells and sit at the edge of the bench; with a neutral (hammer) grip (palms facing each other) bring the dumbbells to your knees, then carefully lie back and use your knees to help guide the weights into starting position beside your lower chest.
Start with your upper arms roughly 45 degrees from your torso and elbows slightly below bench level; wrists neutral and dumbbells aligned over the lower chest.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells under control until your upper arms are just below parallel to the bench or the dumbbells are close to the lower chest/rib area.
Exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together, maintaining the neutral grip, until your arms are nearly extended—do not lock the elbows aggressively.
Keep your core engaged and glutes on the bench to avoid arching the lower back; maintain a steady, controlled tempo on both eccentric and concentric phases.
Avoid flaring the elbows out perpendicular to the body; keep them slightly tucked to reduce shoulder stress and emphasize the pectorals.
Use an appropriate weight you can control for the desired reps; if using heavy loads, have a spotter help lift the dumbbells into and out of the start position.
exercises.description: A decline dumbbell hammer press is performed lying supine on a decline bench while pressing dumbbells with a neutral (palms-facing) grip; the decline angle emphasizes the lower portion of the pectorals while the neutral grip reduces shoulder strain. Controlled lowering and pressing works the chest through a full range while triceps and anterior delts assist and forearms maintain grip.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width) with fingers splayed for stability; extend your legs and balance on your toes so your body forms a straight line from head to heels (high-plank position).
Brace your core and glutes to keep hips level and maintain a neutral neck (look slightly forward or down).
Inhale, then begin lowering by bending the elbows outward (elbows will flare more than in a close-grip push-up) while keeping the shoulders down and away from the ears.
Lower until your chest is close to the floor or your elbows reach about 90 degrees, keeping the body rigid and avoiding hip sag or excessive lumbar arch.
Pause briefly at the bottom with tension in the pectorals and serratus anterior, then exhale and press through the palms to straighten the arms and return to the starting high-plank position.
Keep controlled tempo, maintain full-body tension, and repeat for the desired repetitions. To reduce difficulty, perform the same movement from the knees with hands placed wide.
exercises.description: A prone bodyweight push-up variation with the hands placed wider than shoulder width to increase chest (pectorals) activation. From a high-plank position you lower the torso toward the floor and push back up while maintaining a straight body line.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back (supine on an incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability; bring the dumbbells up to shoulder height with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly (pinch the shoulder blades together) to stabilize the chest and protect the shoulders.
Begin the repetition by slowly lowering the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight elbow bend throughout; lower until you feel a comfortable stretch in the upper chest.
Keep the movement controlled and avoid dropping the weights below the plane of the chest or allowing the elbows to fully lock or flare excessively.
Drive the dumbbells back up by horizontally adducting the shoulders (bringing the arms together) until they meet or nearly meet above the sternum; exhale as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then inhale and repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a neutral neck and avoid shrugging the shoulders; use a weight that allows strict form and full control through both eccentric and concentric phases.
exercises.description: Performed lying back on an inclined bench, the dumbbell incline fly isolates the upper portion of the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from open to together while maintaining a slight elbow bend. It emphasizes pectoral horizontal adduction with shoulder stabilization from the delts and grip support from the forearms.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

exercises.name: Cable Half Kneeling Single Arm Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Assume a half‑kneeling position beside a bench: the foot of the working side is planted flat on the floor in front, the opposite knee rests on the floor (or pad), and the non‑working hand rests lightly on the bench for support.
Reach forward with the working arm and grasp the handle with a neutral grip, arm extended and shoulder relaxed; maintain an upright torso and neutral spine with core engaged.
Initiate the movement by retracting the scapula and driving the elbow back and down, pulling the handle toward your hip/ribcage — lead with the elbow rather than the hand.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive rotation.
Slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control to fully lengthen the lats; maintain core tension throughout.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you return; perform the desired reps then switch sides to repeat on the opposite leg/arm.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable row performed from a half‑kneeling position that emphasizes unilateral lat activation while limiting torso rotation. The working arm pulls the handle back toward the ribcage while the non‑working arm/braced knee provide stability.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a straight or easy-curl bar to a low pulley.
instructions: Setup: Attach a straight or easy-curl bar to a low pulley. Stand facing the machine with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart.

Grip and position: Reach forward and grasp the bar with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width. Hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward (~30–60° from vertical), keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a neutral spine and neck.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to set your scapulae before initiating the row.

Execution (pull): Exhale and pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows backward and keeping them close to your body. Focus on initiating the movement with the lats rather than the arms.
Squeeze: At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together and the lats.
Return (eccentric): Inhale and slowly extend the arms under control until they are nearly straight and the weight is felt in the lats, avoiding a sudden drop.
Repetitions and tempo: Use a controlled tempo (e.g., 1–2 sec concentric, 2–3 sec eccentric) and avoid using torso momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Cues and safety: Keep the spine neutral, neck aligned, wrists straight, and avoid rounding the lower back. Reduce load if you must jerk the torso to complete a rep.
exercises.description: Standing bent-over row using a low cable and reverse (underhand) grip to pull the handle toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement due to the supinated grip. Maintain a hip hinge and neutral spine to isolate the back muscles and minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades against the bench.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs, palms facing each other (neutral/hammer grip); use your legs to help rack the dumbbells up so they are at chest level with elbows bent.
Set your elbows slightly tucked (about 30–45 degrees from the torso) and wrists neutral so the dumbbell handles remain vertical.
Inhale, brace your core, then press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until your arms are nearly extended — do not fully lock the elbows — keeping palms facing each other throughout.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the pectorals, then exhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back down to chest level with control.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping the movement smooth, shoulders pinned to the bench, and wrists neutral to avoid excessive strain.
To finish, bring the dumbbells back to your thighs and use your legs to safely return them to the rack or floor.
exercises.description: A flat-bench dumbbell press performed with a neutral (hammer) grip to emphasize the chest while reducing shoulder rotation. Uses a palms-facing-each-other position to target the pectorals with added triceps and anterior delt involvement.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width) with fingers splayed for stability; extend your legs and balance on your toes so your body forms a straight line from head to heels (high-plank position).
Brace your core and glutes to keep hips level and maintain a neutral neck (look slightly forward or down).
Inhale, then begin lowering by bending the elbows outward (elbows will flare more than in a close-grip push-up) while keeping the shoulders down and away from the ears.
Lower until your chest is close to the floor or your elbows reach about 90 degrees, keeping the body rigid and avoiding hip sag or excessive lumbar arch.
Pause briefly at the bottom with tension in the pectorals and serratus anterior, then exhale and press through the palms to straighten the arms and return to the starting high-plank position.
Keep controlled tempo, maintain full-body tension, and repeat for the desired repetitions. To reduce difficulty, perform the same movement from the knees with hands placed wide.
exercises.description: A prone bodyweight push-up variation with the hands placed wider than shoulder width to increase chest (pectorals) activation. From a high-plank position you lower the torso toward the floor and push back up while maintaining a straight body line.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
Sit upright with a slight lean back (about 10–20 degrees), chest up, and scapulae slightly retracted; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Inhale, brace your core, then exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back until the bar reaches your upper chest or collarbone level.
Squeeze your lats and upper back at the bottom of the motion while keeping wrists neutral and avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Slowly control the weight back to the start by allowing the arms to extend fully overhead while maintaining scapular tension (do not fully relax shoulders between reps).
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return, and avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a supinated (reverse/underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. Pull the bar from an overhead position down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and shoulders retracted.

exercises.name: Cable Close Grip Front Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench and secure your thighs under the pads so your torso cannot rise during the movement (seated position).
Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
Start with arms extended overhead, chest up and a slight lean backward from the hips (about 10–20°) to put tension on the lats (slight recline).
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows down and back, leading with the elbows rather than the hands, and pull the bar to the upper chest/upper sternum.
At the bottom of the movement, squeeze your lats and upper back for a brief 1–2 second pause while keeping the torso stable and not using momentum.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start in a controlled manner until your lats are under tension again (full but not locked extension).
Repeat for the desired number of reps; keep core engaged and avoid excessive swinging or pulling with the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a close grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi while minimizing shoulder abduction; a narrow grip increases biceps and upper-back involvement. Performed seated with thigh pads to stabilize the body while pulling the handle to the upper chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Lever High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present).
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present). Adjust seat so handles are at or slightly above shoulder height when arms are extended.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended and shoulders relaxed. Take a full breath and brace your core.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the handles down and back toward the upper chest/near clavicle, leading with the elbows and keeping them at roughly shoulder height (elbows flared).
Squeeze the shoulder blades and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1 second, keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive trunk lean or momentum.
Slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control (eccentric phase), maintaining tension in the upper back and preventing the weights from slamming.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out during the pull and inhale during the release.

Common form tips: keep the neck neutral, avoid shrugging the shoulders up, don’t use excessive body swing, and adjust seat/handle height so the pull targets the upper back rather than purely the lats.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded high row where you pull overhead/forward handles toward the upper chest to emphasize the upper back (rhomboids, mid traps and rear delts) while also engaging lats and arm muscles. The torso is stabilized against a pad and movement is performed in a controlled pull-and-release.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
Stand centered between the towers in an upright position with feet shoulder-width apart or one foot slightly staggered for stability.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip so the cables run low behind your legs; step forward slightly to create tension in the cables and keep a tall chest.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout the exercise (soft elbows) and a small forward lean from the hips; do not round the shoulders.
Exhale and pull both handles upward and inward in a controlled wide arc until your hands meet or nearly meet at chest level, squeezing the pectorals at the end of the motion.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, keeping the shoulders down and scapulae stable (use serratus anterior to stabilize the shoulder blades).
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement, allowing the cables to return your arms to the start position with control — do not let the weights slam.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining posture, a consistent elbow angle, and steady tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly performed with the pulleys set low; you pull each handle upward and inward in a wide arc to bring the hands together in front of the chest, emphasizing horizontal adduction to work the pectorals. Maintain a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo throughout the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Lever Supinated Grip Seated Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the desired plates and set the chest pad/seat so your chest rests against the pad with arms fully extended when you reach the handles; sit upright with feet braced on the platform or footrests.
instructions: Load the desired plates and set the chest pad/seat so your chest rests against the pad with arms fully extended when you reach the handles; sit upright with feet braced on the platform or footrests.
Grasp the handles with a supinated (palms-up/underhand) grip, wrists neutral and shoulder blades relaxed forward at the start.
Brace your core, keep your chest on the pad and maintain a neutral spine (no excessive arching or leaning forward).
Exhale and pull the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows straight back—lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and feel the contraction in your lats and upper back at the end range; keep the wrists and forearms steady.
Pause for a brief second at peak contraction while maintaining an upright, chest-supported position.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms back to the starting position with controlled movement until your elbows are almost straight, keeping tension on the muscles.
Repeat for the prescribed reps; avoid using torso momentum or excessive shoulder elevation—focus on controlled scapular retraction and full range.
exercises.description: Seated row performed on a plate-loaded lever machine using a supinated (underhand) grip; the movement pulls the handles toward the torso to drive horizontal shoulder extension and scapular retraction. It emphasizes the lats while also loading the arms and upper back musculature.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying One Arm Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying One Arm Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand at chest level with a firm grip (palm facing forward or neutral); place your free hand on your chest or the bench for balance.
Retract your shoulder blade slightly on the working side and brace your core to prevent your torso from rotating.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight up until the arm is nearly extended, keeping a soft bend in the elbow (do not fully lock out).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the chest level, keeping the elbow at about a 45-degree angle relative to the torso.
Maintain steady posture and avoid twisting; reset your scapula and core between reps if needed.
Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat with equal control and setup.
exercises.description: A single-arm dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench to train the pectorals unilaterally while the triceps and delts assist; the opposite hand rests for stability. Emphasizes controlled pressing and core stabilization to prevent torso rotation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
Stand facing across from the pulley in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) with a slight forward lean at the hips; engage your core and keep a neutral spine.
Reach up and grab the handle with the working hand using a neutral grip; allow the arm to start out to the side with a small bend in the elbow.
Depress and stabilize your shoulder (avoid shrugging) and pull the handle down and across your body in a smooth arc toward the opposite mid-chest, rotating the torso slightly as needed.
At the end of the movement, actively squeeze the pectorals and serratus anterior while maintaining the elbow bend; pause briefly at peak contraction.
Slowly and under control, return the handle to the starting position allowing the chest to stretch but keeping tension on the cable; do not let the weight stack slam.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Keep breathing steadily (exhale as you pull across, inhale as you return).
Maintain a slight elbow bend throughout, avoid shoulder hyperextension, and do not use excessive torso rotation or momentum to drive the movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable crossover isolates one side of the chest by pulling a cable across the body from a high pulley, emphasizing the pectoral contraction and allowing a strong end-range squeeze. It is performed standing with a slight forward lean and a controlled return to the start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Low Fly
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in one hand at your side (low position) with a neutral grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in one hand at your side (low position) with a neutral grip.
Hinge very slightly at the hips so your torso tilts forward a few degrees to allow a clear arc for the arm; keep the spine neutral and core braced.
Keep a soft bend in the elbow throughout the movement and the shoulder blades stable (no excessive shrugging).
Initiate the movement from the shoulder/pecs and sweep the dumbbell upward and across your body in a wide arc toward chest/shoulder height, leading with the elbow, not the wrist.
At the top of the arc, pause briefly and squeeze the pectorals while keeping the rest of the body still.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, lowering the dumbbell back to the low starting position until the pecs are mildly stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, then switch sides; avoid twisting the torso, using momentum, or fully locking the elbow.
exercises.description: A single-arm standing dumbbell fly performed from a low starting position, moving the arm in an upward arc to bring the weight across the body and emphasize the pectoral fibers. Controlled eccentric lowering and a slight elbow bend keep tension on the chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Pin Bench Press Conventional grip
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).
instructions: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).

Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor, and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress the shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a conventional shoulder-width (slightly wider than shoulder) overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Begin with the bar resting on the pins (arms either slightly bent or fully extended depending on your setup). Brace your core and take a full breath in before initiating the press.
Press the bar upward from the pins in a controlled, explosive concentric motion until the arms are fully extended and elbows locked out while exhaling.
Lower the bar under control along the same path until it gently contacts the pins at the set height (do not bounce the bar off the pins), pause as needed on the pins to reset tension.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining scapular retraction, a stable foot drive, and controlled tempo on the descent.
exercises.description: A bench press variation performed inside a rack where the bar starts and finishes on safety pins; it emphasizes the press from a dead stop to overload the pectorals through the concentric phase. Performed with a conventional (shoulder‑width) grip to target the mid-chest while the triceps and anterior delts assist.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your chest firmly against the pad, feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent; adopt a tall, neutral spine.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction) and driving your elbows straight back.
Continue pulling until the handles approach your lower ribs/abdomen while squeezing the lats and upper back; avoid shrugging or excessive elbow flare.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start while allowing the shoulder blades to protract, controlling the weight on the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping motion smooth and avoiding torso rocking, momentum, or rounding of the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated lever row on a chest-supported machine that pulls a handle toward the torso, primarily targeting the lats and middle back while also engaging the arms and rear shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

exercises.name: Cable Half Kneeling Single Arm Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Assume a half‑kneeling position beside a bench: the foot of the working side is planted flat on the floor in front, the opposite knee rests on the floor (or pad), and the non‑working hand rests lightly on the bench for support.
Reach forward with the working arm and grasp the handle with a neutral grip, arm extended and shoulder relaxed; maintain an upright torso and neutral spine with core engaged.
Initiate the movement by retracting the scapula and driving the elbow back and down, pulling the handle toward your hip/ribcage — lead with the elbow rather than the hand.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive rotation.
Slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control to fully lengthen the lats; maintain core tension throughout.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you return; perform the desired reps then switch sides to repeat on the opposite leg/arm.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable row performed from a half‑kneeling position that emphasizes unilateral lat activation while limiting torso rotation. The working arm pulls the handle back toward the ribcage while the non‑working arm/braced knee provide stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Cable Lying Fly
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a light–moderate weight you can control through the full range.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a light–moderate weight you can control through the full range.
Lie supine (face up) on a flat bench placed between the two pulley towers with feet flat on the floor for stability.
Reach down and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing position; start with arms out to the sides at about shoulder height and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (squeeze the shoulder blades together and down) to stabilize the shoulders before beginning each rep.

Exhale and bring both hands up and together in a wide arc over your chest, maintaining the slight elbow bend and focusing on squeezing the pectorals at the top; hands should meet or nearly meet above the sternum.

Inhale and slowly lower the handles back out to the starting position in a controlled manner until you feel a gentle stretch across the chest — do not let the elbows drop excessively behind the line of the shoulders.
Keep the torso stable and avoid arching the lower back; maintain control of the cables throughout the entire set and adjust weight if you cannot control the return.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then carefully release the handles and sit up before changing weight or leaving the bench.
exercises.description: A supine cable fly performed on a flat bench using low-set pulleys to isolate the chest by bringing the hands together in a wide arc. The movement emphasizes horizontal shoulder adduction while keeping a slight bend in the elbows to protect the joints.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
