exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.
instructions: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower, hands directly in front of hips and the bar resting against the thighs (standing position).
Keeping the torso vertical, initiate the movement by driving the elbows up and out while pulling the bar close to the body.
Continue lifting until the bar reaches approximately collarbone/upper-chest level or until elbows are roughly shoulder height; lead the movement with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and chest tall, avoiding excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, fully extending the arms but maintaining tension in the delts and traps.
Breathe out while lifting and inhale while lowering. Use a controlled range — avoid lifting the elbows higher than shoulder level to reduce shoulder impingement risk.
exercises.description: A standing barbell upright row that primarily targets the delts by elevating the elbows and pulling the bar toward the upper chest, with significant assistance from the traps and the arm muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and a slight bend in the elbows.
Keep your torso still and shoulders down (do not shrug) throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by leading with the elbows until your arms are approximately parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a slight elbow bend and neutral wrist alignment.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding swinging or leaning the torso to lift heavier weights.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell lateral raises isolate the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides from a seated, back-supported position. Performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to emphasize the shoulder without using momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (not kneeling or prone) with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms facing body/slightly toward you) grip, arms extended at your sides.
instructions: Start standing upright (not kneeling or prone) with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms facing body/slightly toward you) grip, arms extended at your sides.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back, and keep your chest up and torso vertical throughout the movement.
Initiate the lift by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body so they travel in a straight line near the front of your thighs to chest level.

Continue pulling until your elbows reach about shoulder height or slightly above, with elbows higher than your wrists; avoid lifting the dumbbells higher than shoulder level to reduce shoulder impingement risk.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and traps, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control.
Keep the wrists neutral (avoid excessive bending), avoid excessive torso swing or leaning back, and do not use momentum—focus on lifting with the shoulders and upper back.
Choose a weight that allows you to perform the set with good form for the desired reps; if you feel excessive shrugging or shoulder pain, reduce weight or limit range of motion.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Single Delt Row
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine at or slightly above shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine at or slightly above shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight forward lean from the hips, core braced and knees soft.
Reach across with one arm and grasp the handle with a neutral/overhand grip; the working arm should start extended with tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by retracting the scapula and pulling the elbow up and back so the upper arm moves roughly parallel to the floor and toward the ear/upper chest.
Keep the elbow flared slightly outward (not tucked tightly to the side) to emphasize the posterior delt; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
Avoid rotating the torso or shrugging the opposite shoulder; maintain a stable torso and steady foot position throughout the set.
Squeeze the posterior delt/upper back at the top of the pull for 1 second, then slowly control the cable back to the start position on a 2–3 second eccentric.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric). Switch sides and repeat for the desired reps.

Performs controlled concentric pull and a slow eccentric release to load the rear delt and upper back.

Keep your chest up and back neutral (avoid excessive lower-back arch) and prepare to press by rotating your wrists outward so your palms will face forward at the top of the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells overhead while simultaneously rotating your palms until the arms are extended overhead and palms face forward; do not lock or hyperextend the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights controlled overhead, maintaining tension in the delts and triceps.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled manner while rotating the wrists back to the starting position with palms facing your torso and elbows returning to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding using leg drive or excessive torso lean to lift the weights.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Push Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (palms facing each other or slightly forward), elbows under or slightly in front of the dumbbells; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
instructions: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (palms facing each other or slightly forward), elbows under or slightly in front of the dumbbells; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Initiate a quick dip by bending the knees ~10–20 degrees and keeping the torso upright — this is a short, controlled knee bend, not a deep squat.
Explosively extend the hips and knees (drive through the legs and glutes) while simultaneously pressing the dumbbells overhead in one continuous motion.
At the top, fully extend the arms overhead with the dumbbells roughly in line with the ears, shoulders packed and core engaged; avoid excessive lower-back arching.
Lower the dumbbells under control back to shoulder height while absorbing force with slightly bent knees, reset your position if needed, and repeat.
Breathing: inhale before the dip, exhale as you drive and press; choose a weight that allows controlled technique and limit leg drive if the goal is strict shoulder strength.

Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Y Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45 degrees above parallel (bent-over position) with a slight bend in the knees; keep a neutral spine and neck (look at a point on the floor a few feet ahead).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a light dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other (neutral grip).

Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45 degrees above parallel (bent-over position) with a slight bend in the knees; keep a neutral spine and neck (look at a point on the floor a few feet ahead).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with elbows soft (slight bend) and shoulders relaxed away from the ears.

Initiate the movement by externally rotating the shoulders and lifting the dumbbells overhead and outward into a Y shape (hands move diagonally away from the body at about a 30–45° angle from the torso) until arms are roughly in line with the torso or slightly above shoulder level.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top without shrugging; keep the core braced to prevent low-back extension.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting hanging position, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired reps; choose light weights to maintain strict form and avoid momentum or excessive back arching.
exercises.description: A bent-over Y raise is a standing hip-hinged exercise that lifts light dumbbells in a Y shape to train the rear and lateral delts while improving upper-back stability and scapular control. It emphasizes controlled scapular retraction and external rotation through the shoulder joint.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Arnold Press (version 2)
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Standing Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand inside the trap bar with feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward; grip the neutral handles with both hands and stand tall.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Standing Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand inside the trap bar with feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward; grip the neutral handles with both hands and stand tall.
instructions: Stand inside the trap bar with feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward; grip the neutral handles with both hands and stand tall.
Keep a slight bend in the knees, chest up, core braced and spine neutral; arms should remain straight with elbows relaxed.
Exhale and elevate your shoulders straight upward toward your ears, imagining trying to touch the shoulders to the ears; avoid rolling the shoulders forward or backward.
Squeeze the traps at the top of the movement for 1–2 seconds to maximize contraction.
Inhale and lower the shoulders slowly and under control back to the starting position until traps are fully lengthened.
Keep the bar close to your body and maintain neutral wrist and elbow position throughout; do not use back extension or arm pull to lift the weight.
Repeat for desired reps, using a weight that allows controlled, full-range shrugs without momentum.
exercises.description: Standing shrug performed with a trap bar to target the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a neutral-grip handle; focuses on a vertical lifting motion with minimal arm bend. Use a controlled contraction and descent to maximize trap activation.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable 30–45° incline), keeping a neutral spine and head aligned with your spine.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable 30–45° incline), keeping a neutral spine and head aligned with your spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable 30–45° incline), keeping a neutral spine and head aligned with your spine.
Reach down and grasp the barbell with an underhand (palms facing up) grip about shoulder-width apart; tighten your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbows back and pulling the barbell toward your lower chest/upper abdomen, keeping elbows close to your sides to emphasize the lats.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulder blades squeezed together and the chest slightly elevated, avoiding excessive spine extension or kipping.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting hang under control until arms are nearly extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the bar up and inhale as you lower it; keep knees slightly bent and avoid rounding the lower back during the set.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row using an underhand (reverse) grip that emphasizes the lats while also recruiting the biceps and upper back musculature. Performed from a hinged, kneebent standing position and lifting the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and place a loaded barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip (wider than shoulder-width) and let your arms hang fully extended.
Brace your core, inhale, then pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows out and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders retracted, then exhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner until arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or spinal rounding; keep knees slightly bent and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed with a wide overhand grip that emphasizes the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. The torso stays hinged forward while the hips and spine stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Behind The Back Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain a slight bend in the knees with a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Without bending your elbows, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling the shoulders forward or backward.
Hold the top position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the traps hard at peak contraction.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position, allowing the traps to fully stretch before the next rep.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug with the bar held in front of the thighs; raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to target the upper trapezius, pause at the top, then lower back down under control. This is a fundamental trap-building exercise that emphasizes scapular elevation.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shrug
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
instructions: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
Keeping your torso upright and core braced, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears without bending the elbows or rolling the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the trapezius muscles at peak contraction.
Lower the barbell slowly and under control, allowing a full stretch of the traps at the bottom before the next rep.
Exhale as you shrug up and inhale as you lower; maintain strict form throughout all repetitions.
exercises.description: A seated barbell shrug isolates the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a barbell; performing it seated reduces leg drive so the traps take most of the load. It emphasizes strong, vertical shoulder elevation (scapular elevation) with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width so elbows can travel out to the sides.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades, maintaining a stable torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows outward and up, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/upper abdomen while keeping the elbows high to emphasize the rear delts.
At the top, pause briefly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the bar under control back to the start position, fully extending the arms while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row variation that specifically emphasizes the rear delts by rowing with elbows flared out to the sides. Performed from a hip-hinged standing position to target the posterior shoulder and upper back.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the handles with a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and the forearms relatively fixed; start with the arms hanging at your sides.
Exhale and raise your upper arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your elbows reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep your torso still and avoid leaning or using momentum; do not excessively shrug the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with controlled movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining form throughout.
exercises.description: Seated machine lever lateral raise isolates the lateral delts by abducting the arms out to the sides against resistance, emphasizing controlled movement and minimal torso motion. It targets shoulder width and middle deltoid development.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and lift the bar by driving your elbows out to the sides and slightly back, squeezing the rear delts at the top.
Raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or slightly below shoulder height, pause briefly.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Standing upright rear delt raise using a barbell held behind the body. The bar is lifted by driving the elbows out and back to target the posterior deltoids and upper back while maintaining an upright torso position.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
With arms relatively straight, lift the barbell backward and upward away from your body by squeezing the rear delts.
Raise the bar as high as comfortable while maintaining control, then lower slowly back to the starting position.
Avoid leaning forward or using momentum; focus on controlled movement through the rear deltoids.
exercises.description: A standing barbell exercise performed with the bar held behind the body using an overhand grip. The movement involves lifting the barbell backward and upward away from the glutes by extending the shoulders, keeping arms relatively straight to target the posterior deltoids and traps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell over mid-foot. Grip the bar just outside your knees with an overhand grip, chest up and core braced.
Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
Quickly drop under the bar and rotate elbows forward to catch the barbell in a front-rack position on your shoulders, absorbing with a partial squat.
Stand tall from the front-rack position, then press the bar overhead by extending your arms until elbows are fully locked out.
Lower the bar back to the front-rack position, then return it to the floor with control by hinging at the hips and bending the knees.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift combining a barbell clean (lifting the bar to a front-rack position) followed immediately by a press overhead, training the shoulders along with legs, hips and upper back. It develops explosive power, pressing strength, and full-body coordination.

exercises.name: Landmine Shoulders Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neutral spine, and position the bar angled away from you so it can travel upward without hitting your head.
Inhale, then press the handles upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your arms are fully extended overhead or slightly behind the head, avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and elbows unlocked but not hyperextended.
Exhale as you slowly lower the handles back down to the starting shoulder-height position under control, maintaining tension in the delts and core.
Repeat for the desired reps; keep movement smooth and controlled, avoid leaning the torso excessively back or using lower-body drive to move the load.
Adjust stance or hand position to shift emphasis (wider grip = more lateral delt emphasis; closer grip = more anterior delt/pectorals) and lower the weight if you cannot maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A standing landmine press using a Viking press attachment that emphasizes overhead pressing from the shoulders; the movement drives the handles upward from shoulder height to full arm extension, targeting the delts while the triceps and upper chest assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width so elbows can travel out to the sides.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades, maintaining a stable torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows outward and up, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/upper abdomen while keeping the elbows high to emphasize the rear delts.
At the top, pause briefly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the bar under control back to the start position, fully extending the arms while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row variation that specifically emphasizes the rear delts by rowing with elbows flared out to the sides. Performed from a hip-hinged standing position to target the posterior shoulder and upper back.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the handles with a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and the forearms relatively fixed; start with the arms hanging at your sides.
Exhale and raise your upper arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your elbows reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep your torso still and avoid leaning or using momentum; do not excessively shrug the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with controlled movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining form throughout.
exercises.description: Seated machine lever lateral raise isolates the lateral delts by abducting the arms out to the sides against resistance, emphasizing controlled movement and minimal torso motion. It targets shoulder width and middle deltoid development.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and lift the bar by driving your elbows out to the sides and slightly back, squeezing the rear delts at the top.
Raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or slightly below shoulder height, pause briefly.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Standing upright rear delt raise using a barbell held behind the body. The bar is lifted by driving the elbows out and back to target the posterior deltoids and upper back while maintaining an upright torso position.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and knees slightly soft, holding a barbell overhead with a narrow grip (hands close together).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and knees slightly soft, holding a barbell overhead with a narrow grip (hands close together).
Press the barbell overhead so it is directly above your head, then slowly tilt the bar back so it can be lowered behind your head while keeping the upper arms stationary and vertical.
Bend only at the elbows to lower the barbell behind your head until forearms approach the back of the upper arms (controlled stretch in the triceps).
Pause briefly at the bottom without moving the upper arms, then exhale and extend the elbows to press the barbell back up to the overhead start position.
Keep elbows pointing forward and close to each other (do not let them flare), brace your abs and avoid arching the lower back throughout the movement.
Control the tempo on both lowering and lifting phases; use a weight that allows strict form and steady breathing.
exercises.description: Standing barbell overhead triceps extension where the lifter lowers a barbell behind the head by flexing the elbows, then extends the elbows to return to the start, isolating the triceps. The shoulders and core remain stable to control movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged.
Hold a barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart; arms fully extended and the bar resting against your thighs.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back and down, and elbows pinned close to your sides throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows until the forearms are vertical or the bar reaches just below chest level; avoid rotating the wrists or swinging the torso.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the forearms and biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to full arm extension under control, maintaining the pronated grip and stable posture.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and keeping movement isolated to the elbows (no excessive body sway).
exercises.description: Standing barbell reverse curl performed with an overhand (pronated) grip to emphasize the forearm extensors and brachioradialis while also recruiting the biceps. The movement curls the bar upward by flexing the elbows and lowers it with control.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it straight overhead until your arms are fully extended; this is the start position.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing forward and close to your head, maintaining a stable shoulder position.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary until your forearms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly, then exhale and press the barbell back up by extending the elbows to return to the fully extended overhead position while keeping the core braced.
Avoid excessive lower-back arching or letting the elbows flare out; if necessary use lighter weight or a spotter to maintain safe form.
After completing your set, have a spotter help lower the bar to your chest/thighs before removing it from your lap, or carefully rack the bar with assistance.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead triceps extension is a single-joint exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows from an overhead position while the upper arms remain stationary. It emphasizes the long head of the triceps and requires core and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
instructions: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
Place feet flat on the floor and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip so your upper arms lie flat against the pad and wrists are neutral.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above full extension (do not lock the elbows); take a full breath in to brace the torso.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder or torso movement.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a strong biceps contraction (bar near chest/shoulder level), then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with controlled tension.
Keep the movement strict—no swinging, jerking, or lifting the elbows off the pad—and stop short of joint hyperextension at the bottom.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, adjusting grip width to slightly vary biceps emphasis as needed.
exercises.description: Seated on a preacher (incline) bench with upper arms supported on the pad, curl a barbell upward by flexing the elbows to isolate the biceps, then lower under control to stretch the muscle.

exercises.name: Barbell Prone Incline Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a barbell on the floor or a low rack within reach.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a barbell on the floor or a low rack within reach.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench so your chest rests on the pad and your feet/braced on the floor for stability; allow your arms to hang straight down toward the bar.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip and keep your wrists neutral.
Keep your torso pressed into the bench and your upper arms fixed; avoid moving the shoulders or upper body during the lift.
Exhale and curl the barbell by flexing the elbows until your forearms approach your biceps, squeezing the biceps at the top of the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell under control back to full elbow extension so the arms hang straight.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum or swinging to preserve isolation of the biceps.
exercises.description: Prone incline barbell curls are performed lying face-down on an inclined bench and curling a barbell upward, isolating the biceps by minimizing body swing and shoulder involvement. The incline position lengthens the biceps at the start and emphasizes the long head during the curl.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Close grip Bench Pres
exercises.notes: Set a flat bench in a rack at a height where you can unrack and rerack the bar safely while lying flat.
instructions: Set a flat bench in a rack at a height where you can unrack and rerack the bar safely while lying flat.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a small natural arch in the low back; eyes under the bar.
Grasp the bar with a close, reverse (supinated) grip — palms facing your head — hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower; wrap thumbs around the bar for safety.
Unrack the bar with the help of a spotter if needed and hold it straight over your chest with elbows locked and forearms vertical.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicular area (just below the collarbone), keeping elbows tucked slightly toward your sides and wrists stacked over forearms.
Pause briefly at the chest without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar back up along the same vertical path until arms are nearly locked out.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining tight shoulders and a neutral wrist; use a spotter and lighter loads until comfortable with the grip and bar path.
After the set, carefully rack the bar with control and the assistance of a spotter if necessary.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press performed with a close, reverse (supinated) grip that emphasizes triceps activation while also engaging the chest and front deltoids. The bar is lowered to the mid-chest area and pressed back to full arm extension with elbows kept closer to the body.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Wrist Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced.
Grasp a barbell with an underhand (palms up) shoulder-width grip and let it hang at thigh level with your arms fully extended and elbows stationary at your sides.
Keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed; avoid bending the elbows or using body swing.
Curl your wrists upward as far as possible, lifting the barbell by flexing the wrists and squeezing the forearms at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for a controlled contraction.
Slowly lower the barbell by extending the wrists until you feel a stretch in the forearms, returning to the start position.
Repeat for desired repetitions while maintaining strict form and preventing momentum from the shoulders or torso.
exercises.description: A standing isolation exercise that works the wrist flexors by flexing the wrists while holding a barbell with an underhand grip. Performed upright, it emphasizes forearm strength and grip endurance.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
