exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Squat
exercises.notes: Stand on the platform with feet about hip‑to‑shoulder width and place the padded belt snugly around your hips/upper pelvis; center the belt so the load pulls evenly.
instructions: Stand on the platform with feet about hip‑to‑shoulder width and place the padded belt snugly around your hips/upper pelvis; center the belt so the load pulls evenly.
Grasp the machine handles or front bar for balance and stand tall with a neutral spine and chest up; unlock or unhook the lever/pin so the weight is free to move.
Inhale and brace your core, then begin the descent by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, keeping your torso upright.
Keep weight distributed through your heels and midfoot, and ensure your knees track over your toes (do not let them collapse inward).
Lower until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth), maintaining a neutral spine and steady control.
Exhale and drive back up through the heels by extending the knees and hips until you return to the upright start position.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, then carefully re‑lock or re‑hook the lever/pin before removing the belt.
exercises.description: A belt‑loaded squat performed on a lever machine that loads the hips while you remain upright on a platform, emphasizing knee extension. It trains the quads primarily while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and calves for stability and drive.

exercises.name: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Set a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff-legged, not fully locked) and brace your core so the spine is neutral; chest up and shoulders back.
Initiate the rep by hinging at the hips: push your hips straight back while keeping the bar close to your legs and maintaining a long, flat back.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner until you feel a deep stretch in the hamstrings (bar will be around mid-shin or just below the knees depending on mobility); do not let the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes to return to a standing position while keeping the slight knee bend.
Exhale as you reach the top, maintain control on the descent, and repeat for the desired number of reps.
Safety notes: reduce range of motion or weight if your lower back rounds, keep the neck neutral (look a few feet ahead), and use straps or lighter loads if grip (forearms) fails before hamstrings.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge movement performed with a barbell and minimal knee bend that lengthens and loads the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back. Keep a neutral spine and the bar close to the legs throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Smith Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a Smith machine: place two raised platforms or blocks under the bar so the balls of your feet can sit on the edge and your heels hang free; load appropriate weight on the bar or leave empty for bodyweight practice.
instructions: Set up a Smith machine: place two raised platforms or blocks under the bar so the balls of your feet can sit on the edge and your heels hang free; load appropriate weight on the bar or leave empty for bodyweight practice.

Stand upright on the platform with feet hip-width and toes pointing forward, heels hanging off the edge; grip the Smith bar with both hands at a comfortable height for balance (bar can be held at waist/hip level or across upper back if preferred and safe).

Unrack the bar (if using it for support) and step back so you are stable, chest up, shoulders back, and core braced; keep knees straight but not locked throughout the movement to emphasize the gastrocnemius.
Slowly raise your heels by pushing through the balls of your feet until you reach full plantarflexion (standing on your toes); squeeze the calves at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Lower your heels in a controlled manner until they drop below the level of the platform to achieve a full stretch of the calves; do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (rise) and inhaling on the eccentric (lower).
When finished, carefully re-rack the bar (if loaded) and step off the platform; always use Smith machine safety stops and choose a weight that allows strict, controlled reps.
exercises.description: A standing calf raise performed inside a Smith machine with the feet on a raised platform so the heels can drop below toes; the movement isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The bar is used for balance/support while you raise and lower the heels in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch (hands pad)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine weight and seat height so the padded lever rests comfortably across your upper chest/forearms when seated and your feet sit flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the machine weight and seat height so the padded lever rests comfortably across your upper chest/forearms when seated and your feet sit flat on the floor.
Sit upright on the seat with knees bent at about 90 degrees, feet shoulder-width apart and planted. Place your forearms/hands on top of the pad or grasp the machine handles if provided.
Start with your torso upright and spine neutral; brace your core and engage the abs before initiating movement.
Exhale and flex the spine, curling your torso forward by bringing your ribcage toward your pelvis. Lead the movement with your sternum rather than pulling with your arms.
Pause for 1 second at the top of the contraction while keeping the motion slow and controlled.
Inhale and slowly return to the upright starting position by extending the spine in a controlled manner; avoid letting the weight drop or using momentum.
Maintain stable hips and legs throughout (do not lift or thrust with the lower body) and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Seated crunch performed on a lever/pad machine that isolates the abdominal muscles by flexing the torso while the forearms rest on the pad. The movement uses controlled spinal flexion to contract the abs and lift the ribcage toward the pelvis.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
instructions: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
Position your feet much wider than shoulder-width with toes pointed out at ~30–45 degrees (sumo stance).
Brace your core, keep chest tall and a neutral spine, and allow a slight forward torso lean while maintaining an upright upper body.

Begin the movement by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, lowering yourself into a squat until your thighs are roughly parallel to the platform or a comfortable depth where knees track over toes.
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and distribute weight through your heels and mid-foot; avoid collapsing the knees inward.
Drive forcefully through the heels and hips to return to standing, fully extending the hips at the top while resisting the lever’s load throughout the motion.
Control the descent on each rep, breathe in on the way down and exhale as you drive up, and perform repetitions with a steady tempo.
exercises.description: A wide-stance (sumo) squat performed with a lever belt/harness attached to a machine; emphasizes hip drive and inner-thigh engagement while reducing load on the spine. Use a wide, turned-out foot position and sit back between the heels, then drive up through the hips.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean Deadlift
exercises.notes: Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar with a double overhand (or mixed) grip slightly outside the knees; hips should be lower than a conventional deadlift but higher than a full squat, torso more upright and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the bar over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly outward; approach the bar from a standing position (not kneeling).

Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar with a double overhand (or mixed) grip slightly outside the knees; hips should be lower than a conventional deadlift but higher than a full squat, torso more upright and chest up.
Set a neutral spine and pull the shoulders slightly down and back to engage the upper back and lats; keep the head in a neutral position (eyes forward).
Take a full breath and brace the core; push the knees out so they track over the toes and keep the bar close to the shins.

Drive through the heels/mid-foot by extending the knees and hips simultaneously in a powerful, vertical movement — focus on knee extension to emphasize the quads while finishing with hip extension to engage glutes and hamstrings.
Keep the bar close to the body, shrug slightly at the top to engage the traps, and stand fully tall without hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the bar under control by pushing the hips back and bending the knees, maintaining a neutral spine; return the bar to the floor with a controlled descent and reset for the next rep.
Avoid rounding the back, letting the bar drift away from the body, or initiating the lift with the hips alone; maintain a coordinated knee and hip drive throughout.
exercises.description: The barbell clean deadlift is a deadlift variation that starts from a lower, more upright position similar to the clean, placing primary emphasis on the quadriceps while also recruiting the posterior chain and upper back. It trains a strong, vertical bar path and powerful knee and hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Romanian Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
Reach down and grasp the machine handles with a neutral, overhand grip; set a small bend in the knees and lift the chest so your spine is neutral (standing position).
Inhale, brace your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips backward (hip hinge) while keeping the bar/lever close to your legs and maintaining a straight neutral spine.

Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid-shin or when torso is near parallel to the floor), keeping the knees soft and not letting them travel forward excessively.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and a neutral neck position (look slightly forward), then exhale and drive the hips forward to return to the upright start position by contracting the hamstrings and squeezing the glutes.
Keep the movement slow and controlled on the descent (eccentric) and avoid rounding the lower back; repeat for the desired number of reps and re-rack/lock the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes the hamstrings while loading the posterior chain. Stand on the platform and hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, lowering the lever while keeping a neutral spine and the load close to the body.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine to the desired load and securely fasten the belt around your hips so the lever is attached and aligned with your center of mass.
instructions: Set the lever machine to the desired load and securely fasten the belt around your hips so the lever is attached and aligned with your center of mass.
Stand on the platform with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, and a slight bend in the knees; keep the chest up and shoulders back (neutral spine).
Brace your core and take a full breath, then begin the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge), allowing the torso to lower while the knees stay softly bent.
Lower until you feel a firm stretch in the hamstrings or your torso is roughly parallel to the floor — do not round the lower back.
Drive your hips forward by contracting the hamstrings and glutes to return to standing, keeping the spine neutral and avoiding hip thrust hyperextension at the top.
Exhale as you stand, control the descent on the next rep, and maintain tension through the forearms and core throughout each repetition.
exercises.description: A hinge-dominant deadlift variation performed with a belt attached to a lever machine that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings and hip extension. The movement uses a controlled hip hinge with a slight knee bend while the belt/load pulls you forward.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to shoulder height and load an appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to shoulder height and load an appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, brace your core and unrack the bar by standing up straight.
Place the working foot flat on the floor under your hips; position the non-working foot crossed behind or with the toe lightly touching the floor for balance (standing position).
Keep the torso upright, knees slightly soft (not locked) on the working leg to avoid hyperextension.
Exhale and raise your heel on the working leg as high as possible by contracting the calf (plantarflexion), squeezing at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the heel back down under control until the ankle is dorsiflexed to the starting position (feel a stretch in the calf).
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one leg, then carefully rack the bar and switch legs to repeat on the other side.
exercises.description: A single-leg standing calf raise performed with a Smith machine bar across the upper back to isolate the calf of the working leg while the other foot is lightly placed for balance. Raise the heel by plantarflexing the ankle and lower under control for a full range of motion.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and legs extended straight out in front of you; place the balls of your feet against the footplate with heels free to move.
Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
Start with ankles in a neutral or slightly dorsiflexed position to feel a stretch in the calves.
Press through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, pointing your toes away from you and squeezing the calves at peak contraction.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, allowing a full stretch in the calves before the next rep.
exercises.description: Lever rotary calf is a machine-based calf raise performed from a seated position with legs extended, isolating plantarflexion to target the calf muscles through a controlled range of motion using a cable-pulley system.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Squat (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up: load the desired plates on the lever carriage, adjust the back pad and shoulder pads so your back and shoulders sit comfortably against them, and unlock the safety catches.
instructions: Set up: load the desired plates on the lever carriage, adjust the back pad and shoulder pads so your back and shoulders sit comfortably against them, and unlock the safety catches.

Position yourself with your back fully supported on the angled pad, shoulders/braced under the shoulder pads, feet hip-width on the footplate roughly mid-foot to high on the toes depending on quad emphasis (move feet slightly higher to involve glutes more).
Brace your core, keep a neutral spine and head against the pad, and ensure knees track over toes (no excessive inward collapse).
Inhale and slowly lower the carriage by bending at the knees and hips until your thighs are about parallel to the footplate (or a comfortable depth), keeping weight through your heels.
Pause briefly at the bottom without letting the lower back round, then exhale and drive through your heels to extend the knees and hips, returning the carriage to the start position.
Do not lock the knees at the top; maintain control and repeat for the desired reps, then carefully re-engage the safety catches before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever (hack) squat performed on an angled back-supported sled that emphasizes knee extension to load the quadriceps. The user pushes the footplate away by extending the knees and hips while the back remains supported against the pad.

exercises.name: Barbell Dimel Deadlift
exercises.notes: Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar with both hands just outside the legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips back (hip hinge) and lower the barbell down the front of your legs toward mid-shin while keeping the bar close to your body.
Keep the neck neutral, shoulders retracted, and do not let the lower back round as you descend; feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward explosively while pulling the bar up along the thighs to a near-lockout (stand tall), squeezing the glutes at the top.
Control the descent and repeat for the desired reps, breathing in on the lower and exhaling as you drive the hips forward.
Use lighter weight and higher reps if performing Dimel-style sets, and stop or reduce range if you feel lower-back rounding or pain.
exercises.description: A barbell hip-hinge exercise performed from a standing position that emphasizes eccentric and concentric work of the hamstrings by lowering the bar toward the shins and driving the hips forward to stand. The movement trains the posterior chain with a short range of motion and controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Smith Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.
instructions: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.

Stand facing the bar (upright standing position), bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar, keeping your chest up and spine neutral; grip the bar just outside the legs with a double overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull shoulders down and back (retract traps slightly) and ensure the bar is close to your shins with shoulders slightly in front of the bar before initiating the lift.
Drive through the heels by extending the hips and knees together, pushing the floor away while keeping a strong hip hinge; keep the bar close to the body along the fixed vertical path.
Continue to extend until hips are fully locked out with hips forward and chest upright; avoid hyperextending the spine at the top.
To lower, hinge at the hips first and push them back while maintaining a neutral spine, then bend the knees once the bar passes the knees and lower it under control to the starting position.
Breathe: inhale and brace before each rep, exhale at or after the top of the lift; use controlled tempo and avoid rounding the back.
Use the Smith machine safety stops for sets to limit range if needed and reduce weight if you cannot maintain neutral spine or bar path.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift variation performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the hip hinge to train the glutes and posterior chain while the fixed bar path adds stability and control. It develops hip extension strength and reinforces safe bar path for beginners or heavier loads.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch (chest pad)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine and select an appropriate weight. Sit upright with your chest pressed firmly against the chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers (knees bent, feet on or near the floor). Grasp the handles at the chest pad if available or place hands lightly on the pad.
Start with your torso upright and spine in a neutral position, shoulders relaxed and neck long. Keep the pelvis stable against the seat (do not thrust the hips forward).
Exhale and contract your abs to flex the spine, bringing your ribcage and chest down toward your pelvis/knees. Use your abdominal muscles to move — do not pull with your arms or yank the handles.
Hold the peak contraction for 1–2 seconds, maintaining control and avoiding rapid jerking or excessive spinal rounding.
Inhale and slowly return the torso to the starting upright position by controlled spinal extension until the chest pad is contacted again. Keep tension in the abs throughout the return.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding momentum or lifting with the legs.
exercises.description: Seated crunch performed on a lever machine with a chest pad that isolates the abdominal muscles by flexing the spine while the torso is supported. The movement emphasizes controlled trunk flexion to train the rectus abdominis and obliques.

exercises.name: Smith Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
Stand upright with feet hip-width apart (toes pointing forward) on a flat surface or on a small block/plate if extra range of motion is desired; keep knees nearly straight with a slight natural bend.
Unrack the bar and stabilize your core and posture; keep chest up and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, producing a full contraction in the calves; pause briefly at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position with control; if using a block, allow the heels to drop slightly below the toes for added stretch.
Complete the desired number of repetitions with a controlled tempo, then carefully re-rack the bar ensuring it is secure before stepping away.
exercises.description: A loaded standing calf raise performed in a Smith machine. The lifter rises onto the balls of the feet to concentrically contract the calf muscles, then lowers with control to work the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Smith Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
Stand upright with feet hip-width apart (toes pointing forward) on a flat surface or on a small block/plate if extra range of motion is desired; keep knees nearly straight with a slight natural bend.
Unrack the bar and stabilize your core and posture; keep chest up and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, producing a full contraction in the calves; pause briefly at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position with control; if using a block, allow the heels to drop slightly below the toes for added stretch.
Complete the desired number of repetitions with a controlled tempo, then carefully re-rack the bar ensuring it is secure before stepping away.
exercises.description: A loaded standing calf raise performed in a Smith machine. The lifter rises onto the balls of the feet to concentrically contract the calf muscles, then lowers with control to work the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Squat
exercises.notes: Setup: Place a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; position a loaded barbell across your upper back (traps), grip the bar just outside shoulder width and stand with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Setup: Place a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; position a loaded barbell across your upper back (traps), grip the bar just outside shoulder width and stand with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades back, and keep your chest up and eyes forward; this is a standing start position.
Begin the descent by sending your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, maintaining an upright torso and keeping knees tracking over your toes.
Lower under control until your glutes lightly touch the bench (do not relax fully or sit down), pause 1 second while maintaining tension in the legs and core.
Drive through your heels and midfoot, extend the knees and hips to stand back up to the starting position while exhaling; keep the bar stable on your upper back throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps. Use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy loads and avoid bouncing off the bench or rounding the lower back.
exercises.description: A back‑loaded squat variation where you lower yourself to lightly tap a bench behind you, emphasizing knee‑dominant loading to target the quadriceps while maintaining an upright torso. The bench enforces a consistent depth and helps with technique for beginners and lifters training tempo.

exercises.name: Lever Romanian Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
Reach down and grasp the machine handles with a neutral, overhand grip; set a small bend in the knees and lift the chest so your spine is neutral (standing position).
Inhale, brace your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips backward (hip hinge) while keeping the bar/lever close to your legs and maintaining a straight neutral spine.

Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid-shin or when torso is near parallel to the floor), keeping the knees soft and not letting them travel forward excessively.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and a neutral neck position (look slightly forward), then exhale and drive the hips forward to return to the upright start position by contracting the hamstrings and squeezing the glutes.
Keep the movement slow and controlled on the descent (eccentric) and avoid rounding the lower back; repeat for the desired number of reps and re-rack/lock the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes the hamstrings while loading the posterior chain. Stand on the platform and hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, lowering the lever while keeping a neutral spine and the load close to the body.

exercises.name: Lever Sitting Toe Raise
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
instructions: Sit on the machine bench with your knees bent at about 90 degrees and the padded lever resting across your lower thighs just above the knees.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
Start with your heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet, plantarflexing the ankles to raise your heels as high as possible while keeping the knees stationary.
Hold the top position for a brief pause, squeezing the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping a controlled tempo and maintaining upright torso and stable knee position throughout.
exercises.description: A seated calf-raise performed on a lever-style machine that isolates the lower leg by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while the knees are bent. It emphasizes the soleus and gastrocnemius to build calf strength and size.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and place it so you can lie face down with your chest supported and feet on the floor for stability.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and place it so you can lie face down with your chest supported and feet on the floor for stability.

Load a barbell with an appropriate weight and place it within reach on the floor or on low blocks; lie prone (face down) on the bench so your torso is supported and you can reach the bar with straight arms.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (palms down) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; keep your neck neutral and eyes down.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades, then pull the barbell up toward the upper chest or lower sternum while keeping the elbows high and flared out to the sides (lead with the elbows).
Squeeze the rear delts and upper back at the top for a brief 1–2 second pause, avoiding excessive shoulder shrugging.
Lower the barbell slowly and under control to full arm extension while maintaining chest support on the bench; do not use torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps, breathing out as you pull and inhaling as you lower. Reduce load if you cannot keep elbows high and maintain strict form.
exercises.description: Lying prone on an incline bench, you row a barbell with elbows flared out to emphasize the rear deltoids and upper back. The chest-supported position removes body swing and isolates the posterior delts and scapular retractors.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.

Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.

Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip wider than shoulder-width and let the bar hang at thigh level.
Keep the chest up, shoulders back, and torso vertical; do not lean forward or excessively arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbows out and upward, keeping the bar close to the body.
Raise the bar to about upper-chest / collarbone height with elbows higher than the wrists; lead with the elbows, not the hands.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulder blades slightly squeezed, avoiding shrug-only motion or lifting the bar above shoulder level.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position to complete one rep.
Keep wrists neutral (not excessively bent) and breathe out on the lift, inhale on the descent.
Use a moderate weight and controlled tempo to avoid shoulder impingement; stop if you feel anterior shoulder pain.
exercises.description: Standing wide-grip upright rows use an overhand, wider-than-shoulder grip to pull a barbell vertically toward the upper chest, emphasizing the lateral delts and upper traps while also engaging the upper back and grip muscles.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Zercher Back Extension
exercises.notes: Set up on a hyperextension / Roman chair: anchor your feet, position the hip pad under the front of your hips/upper thighs so your torso can hinge freely, and face down (prone).
instructions: Set up on a hyperextension / Roman chair: anchor your feet, position the hip pad under the front of your hips/upper thighs so your torso can hinge freely, and face down (prone).
Cradle a barbell in the crook of your elbows (Zercher hold) and keep the bar close to your body; cross your forearms or grip the bar to secure it and keep elbows tucked.
Start with your torso lowered toward the floor until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings and glutes or your body is roughly perpendicular to the pad (hip flexion). Keep a neutral neck and spine.

Drive your hips forward and contract your glutes and hamstrings to raise your torso until your body forms a straight line from head to heels or you reach a neutral spine — do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tight core and glute contraction, then slowly lower back to the start position under control.
Breathe out as you extend up, inhale as you lower. Maintain tension in the hips and avoid bending at the knees or using momentum.
exercises.description: A hip-extension back extension performed on a Roman chair while holding a barbell in the Zercher (cradle of the elbows) position; it emphasizes hip drive to raise the torso and targets the glutes and hamstrings while the erector spinae stabilizes the spine.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Hip Thrust (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
instructions: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
Position the padded roller directly over your hips and place your feet flat on the foot platform about hip-width apart, knees bent.
Begin with your hips lowered so your thighs are angled toward the platform (starting position). Grip the handles for stability.
Drive through your heels and contract your glutes to extend your hips upward until your hips are in line with your shoulders and knees (full hip extension).
Squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping your chin tucked and spine neutral; avoid hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the lever in a controlled manner back to the starting position, allowing the hips to descend fully while maintaining tension in the glutes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (hip raise) and in on the eccentric (lowering) phase.
exercises.description: Plate-loaded lever hip thrust performed from a seated/reclined position to drive the hips upward against resistance, primarily targeting the glute muscles. Feet are planted on a foot platform while a padded lever rests on the hips and the upper back is supported by the backrest.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Lever Alternating Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
Load an appropriate plate weight and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp the right and left lever handles (one in each hand) with a neutral or overhand grip. Begin with both arms extended overhead with a slight bend in the elbows.

For each rep, exhale and pull one lever down and in a smooth arc toward your chest/upper stomach using the shoulder joint — lead with the upper arm, not the wrist, and keep the elbow slightly bent and fixed.
At the bottom of the movement, pause briefly and focus on squeezing the pectorals and lats on the working side while keeping your torso stationary.
Inhale and slowly return the lever under control back to the start (overhead) to complete the eccentric phase; avoid letting the shoulder snap back.
Alternate sides each repetition (right arm then left arm) while maintaining the same tempo, core bracing, and no excessive torso rotation or lumbar arch.
Keep movements controlled, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain a fixed elbow angle and stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover is a single-arm/alternating machine pullover that moves the upper arm in an arc from overhead down toward the chest, emphasizing the pectorals while also recruiting the lats and serratus anterior. The movement is performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Smith Narrow Row
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to a low position (around mid-shin to knee height) and load an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to a low position (around mid-shin to knee height) and load an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the bar with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and grasp the bar with a narrow, overhand (pronated) grip, hands close together.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is at roughly a 30–45° angle to the floor, keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a neutral spine and braced core.
Begin the rep by exhaling and driving your elbows straight back, pulling the bar in a vertical path toward your lower chest/upper abdomen while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows tucked close to the sides; avoid flaring the elbows or using excessive torso momentum.
Pause briefly at the top with the lats contracted, then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with controlled tension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain a steady tempo and neutral spine throughout; reduce weight if you cannot keep form.
exercises.description: A bent-over narrow-grip row performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the lats and mid/upper back by pulling a fixed bar toward the torso while keeping the elbows close to the body. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine provides stability and forces a vertical pull.

exercises.name: Smith Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench inside the Smith machine and adjust safety stops to a height that will catch the bar just above your chest at the bottom of the movement. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a decline bench inside the Smith machine and adjust safety stops to a height that will catch the bar just above your chest at the bottom of the movement.
Load appropriate weight on the Smith bar and position the bench so your head is lower than your hips (decline angle). Anchor your feet under the bench foot pads so your body is stable.
Lie supine on the decline bench with your torso supported, feet secured, and your eyes roughly under the bar. Maintain a neutral spine with a small natural arch in the lower back.
Grip the bar with a pronated (palms away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. Ensure wrists are straight and stacked over elbows.

Unlock the bar from the hooks by lifting slightly and rotating/unhooking as required by the Smith machine, then lower the bar to a position just below the lower chest / lower sternum in a controlled manner.
Keep elbows angled roughly 30–45 degrees from your torso (not flared fully out) and inhale as you lower the bar to the chest.
Pause briefly at the chest without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward using the pectorals and triceps until your arms are nearly extended (do not violently lock the elbows).
Control the descent on the next repetition and repeat for the desired number of reps. After finishing, carefully guide the bar back onto the hooks/safety catches before exiting the bench.
exercises.description: A decline bench press performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the lower portion of the chest by pressing the bar from a declined (head-lower) supine position. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine provides stability while you lower the bar to the lower chest and press back up.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Smith Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load appropriate weight. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a pronated (overhand) or slightly neutral grip, hands just outside hip width. Step back so the bar pulls you slightly forward.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or a slight incline), keep a soft bend in the knees, and maintain a neutral spine (no rounding).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades back and down to initiate the movement.
Row the bar toward your lower ribs/upper abdomen by driving the elbows straight back, keeping elbows close to the body and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the lats and upper back, then lower the bar under control until arms are nearly extended and tension is felt in the back.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while maintaining a strong hip hinge, neutral spine, and steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the lower).
Avoid jerking with the lower back, excessive torso rotation, or letting the hips rise — keep legs and core engaged to stabilize the movement.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the lats and mid/upper back by pulling a fixed barbell toward the torso from a hip-hinged position. The legs and core stabilize the body while the arms and scapular muscles drive the pulling motion.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
Position a flat bench in the machine so the bar will line up with the mid-chest when you are lying down.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a slight natural arch in the low back; retract your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grasp the bar with a medium grip (hands slightly wider than shoulder-width) and ensure wrists are stacked over elbows; keep head on the bench.
Unrack the bar by rotating it or disengaging the safety, then lower the bar under control to the mid-chest/sternum while keeping elbows at about a 30–60° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then press the bar up explosively until arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain a tight core and pressed scapulae throughout the movement for stability; avoid excessive flaring of the elbows or hyperextending the lower back.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar by rotating it back into the safety catches when finished.
exercises.description: A Smith machine variation of the flat bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench, pressing the fixed bar vertically to work the chest. The guided bar path reduces stabilization demand while emphasizing pressing strength in the pectorals.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down.
Sit with your chest pressed into the pad, grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, and keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Start with the arms hanging at your sides (shoulders relaxed) and brace your core to prevent movement of the torso.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (about 90 degrees), keeping the elbows slightly bent and the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while avoiding shoulder shrugging (do not lift with the neck or upper trapezius).
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position under control, maintaining chest contact with the pad and minimizing momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, choosing a weight that allows strict form without swinging.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported lateral raise performed on a lever machine to isolate the lateral deltoids; the chest pad prevents torso swing so the delts do the work. Arms are raised out to the sides with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Squat (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load desired plates and set any safety stops on the lever machine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Load desired plates and set any safety stops on the lever machine.

Sit back against the angled pad (leaning/reclined position) and place your shoulders under the shoulder pads or hold the upper handles; position feet shoulder-width on the footplate with heels firmly planted.
Unlatch or disengage the safety / lock so the sled can move freely.

Brace your core and keep your back pressed into the pad; inhale and slowly bend the knees to lower the sled until your thighs are about parallel to the footplate (or until a controlled depth where knees track over toes).

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the legs and then exhale as you drive through the heels to extend the knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but not locked out.
Control the descent back to the start position for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral head and spine and steady breathing throughout.
When finished, carefully re-engage the safety / latch before releasing the shoulder pads or standing up.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever squat performed on a reclining/hack-style sled where you push a footplate away to extend the knees and hips. The movement emphasizes knee extension under load with the back supported by the angled pad.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps, grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and step back from the rack, then position an elevated platform or weight plate under the balls of your feet so your heels can hang off the edge; stand with feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain an upright torso; keep knees slightly soft (not fully locked) throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, coming up onto your toes and fully contracting the calves.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, keeping the barbell stable on your traps and upper back (traps engaged to support the load).
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the level of the platform until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully step forward and rack the barbell with control or use a spotter/safety pins if needed.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed with a barbell across the upper back; raise heels by plantarflexing at the ankle to work the calf muscles, then lower heels below the platform for a stretch. The barbell and core help stabilize the body during each repetition.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean grip Front Squat
exercises.notes: Take a deep breath, brace your core, and keep your chest up with an upright torso throughout the movement. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, unrack the barbell into a front-rack position with elbows high and the bar resting on your front delts/clavicles.
Take a deep breath, brace your core, and keep your chest up with an upright torso throughout the movement.
Descend by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, keeping knees tracking over toes until thighs reach parallel or slightly below.
Drive through your full foot to stand back up, maintaining high elbows and core tension to prevent the bar from rolling forward.
Complete all reps with control, then carefully re-rack the barbell or lower it safely to the floor.
exercises.description: A barbell front squat performed from a clean (front-rack) grip where the bar rests on the front delts/upper chest; you squat down to roughly parallel and stand back up. This variation emphasizes knee extension, placing primary load on the quads while the upper back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Lever Rear Foot Elevanted Split Squat (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the desired load on the lever machine and adjust the rear foot platform/pad so the top of your rear foot can rest comfortably when standing in position. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the desired load on the lever machine and adjust the rear foot platform/pad so the top of your rear foot can rest comfortably when standing in position.

Stand facing forward in front of the machine and place the top of your rear foot onto the elevated lever/platform so your front foot is planted on the floor in a split stance (front foot roughly 2–3 feet in front of the rear platform).
Grip the machine handles or hold the lever for stability, keep your chest up, shoulders back, and brace your abs; maintain an upright torso throughout.

Slowly lower your body by bending the front knee and hip until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor or the front knee reaches ~90° (rear knee approaching but not touching the platform). Ensure the front knee tracks over the midline of the foot and does not collapse inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press through the front heel to extend the knee and hip and return to the starting upright position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps, then carefully step forward, switch legs, and perform the same number of reps on the opposite side.
exercises.description: A single‑leg split squat performed with the rear foot elevated on a lever/plate-loaded platform; this variation emphasizes the front leg's knee and hip extension to load the quadriceps while challenging single‑leg stability. The movement is performed standing with the rear foot supported on the machine's elevated pad.

exercises.name: Barbell Hook grip Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend knees to grip the bar just outside your legs using a hook grip (thumb wrapped around bar, secured by first two fingers). Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing forward or slightly outward, with the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and bend knees to grip the bar just outside your legs using a hook grip (thumb wrapped around bar, secured by first two fingers).
Brace your core, pull shoulder blades down and back, and maintain a neutral spine with chest up.
Drive through your heels, extending hips and knees simultaneously while keeping the bar close to your body.
Stand tall at the top with hips fully extended and shoulders back, then lower under control by hinging at the hips first.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift performed with a hook grip using a conventional stance that emphasizes hip extension and the posterior chain. The hook grip allows for a stronger hold on the bar without relying on mixed grip, while training powerful hip and knee extension with strong back stability.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.
